## PAGE 00939: S2 Service Internal Structure

- page_id: `136721650`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721650/S2+Service+Internal+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:28.117+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

To create an S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

1. Create Service Roles .
2. Associate Service Roles using Service Connectors.
3. Create Service Exchanges flowing via Service Connectors.

##### Creating Service Exchange in S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

To create a Service Exchangein the S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Service Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_sevice_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange.png'] and then click the Service Connector.
2. Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Service Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Service Exchangedata.

## PAGE 00940: S2 Service Structure

- page_id: `136721634`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721634/S2+Service+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:27.395+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S2 Viewpoint identifies and specifies how services are structured to create higher-aggregated services. To provide high-level views, dependencies to other services, nodes, and resources, as well as service interfaces and service functions can be represented.

**Implementation**

The S2 view can be represented using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the S2 Services Structure diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The S2 Viewpoint identifies and specifies how services are structured to create higher-aggregated services. To provide high-level views, dependencies to other services, nodes, and resources, as well as service interfaces and service functions can be represented.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The S2 view can be represented using the<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ S2 Service Structure diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Sample</strong> </span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="S2 Service Structure" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">An example of the S2 Services Structure diagram.</span></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721666 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00941: S3 Service Interfaces

- page_id: `136721666`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721666/S3+Service+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:28.583+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s3-service-interfaces', 's3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

68833919

68833921

68833920

**Description**

The purpose of the S3 Service Interfaces view is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.

**Implementation**

The S3 view can be represented using:

- S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S3.png']
- S3 MS Word document report.

68833918

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='SYSMLP2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Port' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='UAF12P2024x']

## PAGE 00942: S4 Service Functions

- page_id: `136721668`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721668/S4+Service+Functions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:31:59.237+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s4-service-functions', 's4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824179280

824179282

824179281

**Description**

The S4 Service Functions view defines the behavior of a service in terms of the functions it is expected to perform. S4 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024x'] and R4 - Resource Functions, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.

**Implementation**

The S4 view can be represented using:

- S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram based on the UML Clas diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4.png']

- S4 Service Functions diagram for the Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4_flow.png']
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- UML Class diagram.

824179278

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Control Flow' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='']

## PAGE 00943: S5 Service States

- page_id: `136721674`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721674/S5+Service+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:29.331+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s5-service-states', 's5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824386959

824386961

824386960

**Description**

The purpose of the S5 Service States view is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

The S5 view can be represented using a S5 Service States diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

824386958

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service State Description' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024x']

It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S5 view can be represented using a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ee4b8256-c894-4fc6-aa98-222af27fda31">S5 Service States diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="S5 Service States diagram" ac:alt="S5 Service States diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="S5 Service States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c07d1543-7678-4bab-bd8f-d00b4602d54a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">824386958</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service State Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00944: S6 Service Interactions

- page_id: `136721676`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721676/S6+Service+Interactions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:29.497+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s6-service-interactions', 's6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824439177

824439179

824439178

**Description**

The purpose of S6 Service Interactions view is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='UAF12P2024x']). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

824439176

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='SYSMLP2024x']

## PAGE 00945: S7 Service IF Parameters

## PAGE 00946: S8 Service Parametric

- page_id: `136721683`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721683/S8+Service+Parametric
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:30.292+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S8 Service Policy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

498484258

498484260

498484259

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specifiction. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

498484257

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67eb6d9b-397a-4e8d-9f70-f4bd54e72298"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484257</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721680 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00947: S8 Service Policy

- page_id: `136721680`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721680/S8+Service+Policy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:27:24.552+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s8-service-policy', 's8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824966407

824966409

824966408

**Description**

The purpose of the S8 Service Policy view is to specify constraints that apply to implementations of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of service specifications, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. An S8 product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of must conform.

**Implementation**

The S8 view can be represented using:

- S8 Service Policy table. [ATTACHMENT filename='S8.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S8 Service Parametric' space='UAF12P2024x'] diagram.
- S8 spreadsheet report .

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

824966406

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Policy' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='']

**Related procedures**

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918471926

918471928

918471927

The Security (Sc) domain addresses the security constraints and information assurance attributes that exist on exchanges between resources and Operational Performers. It illustrates the security assets, security constraints, security controls, families, and measures required to address specific security concerns.

918471925

**Views**

## PAGE 00951: Security Connectivity

- page_id: `136722298`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722298/Security+Connectivity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:37:10.968+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918511719

918511721

918511720

**Description**

The Security Connectivity (Sc-Cn) domain addresses the security constraints and information assurance attributes that exist on exchanges across resources and across performers. It lists security exchanges across security assets; the applicable security controls; and the security enclaves that house the producers and consumers of the exchanges. Measurements can optionally be included.

**Implementation**

The Security Connectivity (Sc-Cn) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Role-based Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- Reports (.xlsx): - Security Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet. - Security Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Role-based Connectivity table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722318 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00953: Security Constraints

- page_id: `136722318`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722318/Security+Constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:38:58.939+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918678581

918678584

918678582

**Definition**

The Security Constraints (Sc-Ct) domain shows the security-related policy, guidance, laws and regulations as applicable to resources. It Specifies textual rules/non-functional requirements that are security constraints on resources, information and data (e.g. security-related in the form of rules (e.g. access control policy). A common way of representing access control policy is through the use of XACML (eXtensible Access Control Markup Language), it is expected that implementations of UAF allow users to link security constraints to external files represented in XACML.

**Implementation**

The Security Constraints (Sc-Ct) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Constraints diagram

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

918678580

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723362 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00954: Security Control

- page_id: `136723362`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723362/Security+Control
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:08.409+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The management, operational, and technical control (i.e., safeguard or countermeasure) prescribed for an information system to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the system and its information.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The management, operational, and technical control (i.e., safeguard or countermeasure) prescribed for an information system to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the system and its information.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723363 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00955: Security Control Action

- page_id: `136723363`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723363/Security+Control+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:08.466+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A call of a Security Control in the context of another Security Control. It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset in a particular context.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Call Behavior Action

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset in a particular context. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Call Behavior Action</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Collection of information systems connected by one or more internal networks under the control of a single authority and security policy. The systems may be structured by physical proximity or by function, independent of location.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Class

561814059

561814061

561814060

**Description**

The Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of security elements.

The intended usage of the Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Environment diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Parametric. Operational diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Parametric. Resource diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx)

## PAGE 00962: Security Processes

- page_id: `136722333`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918798584

918798586

918798585

**Description**

The **Security Processes (Sc-Pr)**domain shows the specification of the Security Control families, security controls, and measures required to address a specific security baseline. It provides a set of Security Controls and any possible enhancements as applicable to assets. The activity diagram describes operational or resource level processes that apply (operational level) or implement (resource level) security controls/enhancements to assets located in enclaves and across enclaves. This Security Process domain can be instantiated either as a variant of an activity/flow diagram or as a hierarchical work breakdown structure.

**Implementation**

The Security Processes (Sc-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Processes diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Process Flow (Resources) diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Process Flow (Operational) diagram' space=''] .

918798583

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extensions**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Class</p>
````

## PAGE 00964: Security Structure

- page_id: `136722343`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722343/Security+Structure
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:32:58.403+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918973399

918973401

918973400

**Description**

The Security Structure (Sc-Sr) domain shows the structure of security information and where it is used at the operational and resource level. It captures the allocation of assets (operational and resource, information, and data) across the security enclaves, shows applicable security controls necessary to protect organizations, systems, and information during processing, while in storage (bdd), and during transmission (flows on an ibd). This view also captures Asset Aggregation and allocates the usage of the aggregated information at a location.

**Implementation**

The Security Structure (Sc-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Structure diagram

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919067284

919067286

919067285

**Description**

The Security Taxonomy (Sc-Tx) domain shows the security assets and security enclaves. It defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners that are available to implement security, security constraints (policy, guidance, laws and regulations) and details where they are located (security enclaves).

**Implementation**

The Security Taxonomy (Sc-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024x'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Taxonomy table

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

565418804

565418806

565418805

**Description**

The Security Traceability (Sc-Tr) domain depicts the mapping of a risk to each of the following: risk owner, risk mitigations, and affected asset roles.

**Implementation**

The Security Traceability (Sc-Tr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] shows which risks are applicable to Asset Roles.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Controls to Risk Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix

/><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">shows which risks are applicable to Asset Roles.</span><br /></span></li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_controls_to_risk_mapping.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Security Traceability" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Security Controls to Risk Mapping Matrix</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="risk_to_assets_mapping.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Security Traceability" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fecfb740-ba18-4081-a2b1-229393a18cac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">565418802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722610 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00967: Select Framework Views dialog

- page_id: `136722610`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722610/Select+Framework+Views+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:25.351+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you create a new project, you can easily define your own project structure by choosing only the views that you really need. You can achieve this by using the **Select Framework Views** dialog.

The **Select Framework Views**dialog is displayed when the **Select Framework Views** option in the **New Project** dialog is set to *True*. By default this option is set to *True*. If you want to create a project with the default structure containing all the views, set this option to *False*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Framework Views option in the New Project dialog

In the **Select Framework Views**dialog, you can select which views you want to create in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The dialog for selecting views for your new project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><p>When you create a new project, you can easily define your own project structure by choosing only the views that you really need. You can achieve this by using the <strong>Select Framework Views</strong> dialog.</p><p>The <strong>Select Framework Views </strong>dialog is displayed when the <strong>Select Framework Views</strong> option in the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog is set to <em>True</em>. By default this option is set to <em>True</em>. If you want to create a project with the default structure containing all the views, set this option to <em>False</em>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Select Framework Views dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Select Framework Views option in the New Project dialog</h6><p>In the <strong>Select Framework Views </strong>dialog, you can select which views you want to create in your model. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_framework_views.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Select Framework Views dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The dialog for selecting views for your new project</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720384 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00968: Select Perspective dialog

- page_id: `136720384`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720384/Select+Perspective+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-12-20T12:59:09.901+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the **Select Perspective**dialog appears with the ability to switch between perspectives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Business Analyst | This perspective provides features for Business Analysts. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| Business Architect | This perspective provides features for Business Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| DoDAF 2.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF 2.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF 2.0-related features are hidden. |
| DODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF-related features are hidden. |
| MODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to MODAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-MODAF-related features are hidden. |
| NAF 4.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF 4.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF 4.0-related features are hidden. |
| NAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF-related features are hidden. |
| SOA Engineer | This perspective is a set of roles primarily involved in designing and implementing projects: Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer. This is the default Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Cameo Enterprise Architect configuration. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| Software Architect | This perspective is dedicated to Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer roles that design and implement the project. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| System Analyst | This perspective is a set of roles primarily dedicated to eliciting requirements and modeling the system. Analysis features are highlighted. Code engineering, transformations, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| System Engineer | This perspective provides features dedicated to System Engineers. SysML support is accessible. Requirements elicitation and modeling features are highlighted. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-system-modeling-related features are hidden. |
| TOGAF Architect | This perspective provides features for TOGAF Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| UAF Architect | The perspective provides features dedicated to UAF Architects with all the UAF products available. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-UAF-related features are hidden. |
| EA-BPMN Architect* | This perspective provides features dedicated to Enterprise and IT Architects with the essential elements for this area: capabilities, requirements, operational behaviors (with BPMN), resources (hardware, software, facilities), data, and personnel. The user interface is simplified; advanced UAF elements and diagrams are hidden. |
| Zachman Framework | This Quick Start perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling and not an overpopulated interface for quick learning. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden but easily accessible in the expert mode of this perspective. |
| Customize | Open the Customize Perspectives dialog to customize the selected perspective. You can make your tool interface less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. For example, you can group functional features, and show, or hide selected commands. |

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF-related features are hidden.</td></tr><tr><th>SOA Engineer</th><td>This perspective is a set of roles primarily involved in designing and implementing projects: Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-UAF-related features are hidden. </td></tr><tr><th>EA-BPMN Architect*</th><td>This perspective provides features dedicated to Enterprise and IT Architects with the essential elements for this area: capabilities, requirements, operational behaviors (with BPMN), resources (hardware, software, facilities), data, and personnel. The user interface is simplified; advanced UAF elements and diagrams are hidden. </td></tr><tr><th>Zachman Framework</th><td>This Quick Start perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling and not an overpopulated interface for quick learning. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden but easily accessible in the expert mode of this perspective.</td></tr><tr><th>Customize<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></th><td><p><span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Open the </span><strong>Customize Perspectives </strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">dialog to customize the selected perspective. </span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can make your tool interface less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For example, you can group functional features, and show, or hide selected commands.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><hr /><p>*Starting with the 2024x version, the <strong style="text-align: left;">UAF Enterprise Architecture Project</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>was renamed to<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">EA with BPMN Project.</strong></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722613 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00969: Select Symbol dialog

- page_id: `136722613`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722613/Select+Symbol+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:25.562+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['select-symbol-dialog', 'affiliation', 'frame']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1015178682

1015178684

1015178683

Use **Select Symbol** dialog to apply military symbols for resources.

To open the **Select Symbol** dialog, do either

- Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
- Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
- Open the Specification window of any UAF element. Select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] . The **Select Symbol** dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Select Symbol** dialog consists of these four components:

##### Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree

Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.

##### Quick filter

You can search for symbols in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs by using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy. For example, type "1.X.3.2.2.1.3".
- Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols. For example, type "*Frying" if you want to find "Fixed Wing" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Affiliation area

Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. The basic affiliation categories are unknown, friend, neutral, and hostile. Additionally the pending, suspect, assumed friend, joker and faker affiliations may be applied. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.

##### Frame area

Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.

##### Preview area

You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.

1015178681

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying military symbols' space='UAF12P2024x']

if you want to find &quot;Fixed Wing&quot; somewhere in the hierarchy.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Affiliation area</span><br /></span></h3><p>Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720376 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00970: Selecting Environment

- page_id: `136720376`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720376/Selecting+Environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:33.354+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: ['environment-selection-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment Selection** dialog opens when starting the UAF plugin for the first time.

To select the enterprise architecture framework environment

1. Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build: Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog. This information may help you decide what to choose.
  - UAF Enterprise Architecture
  - UAF
  - DoDAF 2.0
  - MODAF
  - NAF v. 3
  - NAF 4.0
  - BPMN
  - SysML
  - DoDAF 1.5
  - TOGAF
2. Click to select the Expert check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the Expert check box empty.
3. Click the Continue button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).

For information about changing the environment, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks' space='UAF12P2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Environment Selection</strong> dialog opens when starting the UAF plugin for the first time.</p><p>To select the enterprise architecture framework environment</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build:<br /><ul><li>UAF Enterprise Architecture</li><li>UAF</li><li>DoDAF 2.0</li><li>MODAF</li><li>NAF v. 3</li><li>NAF 4.0</li><li>BPMN</li><li>SysML</li><li>DoDAF 1.5</li><li>TOGAF</li></ul>Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog. This information may help you decide what to choose.</li><li>Click to select the <strong>Expert</strong> check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the <strong>Expert</strong> check box empty.</li><li>Click the <strong>Continue</strong> button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c3a5619-bc24-48cf-9e54-25b1c53712c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about changing the environment, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722616/Service+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:25.779+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Service Assignment Wizard** is used to define Service for the Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.

To open the **Service Assignment Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] diagram (UAF)
2. On the diagram pane, right-click the Version Of Configuration and select Service . The Service Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Service selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create new Service that will be assigned for Version Of Configuration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Service for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the Select Service step, click the Creation Mode button.
2. Select package where you want to create a Service.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Service, type its name.
5. Click Close .

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Service is being used in Version Of Configuration.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Service stops being used in Version Of Configuration.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723377 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00980: Service Exchange Kind

- page_id: `136723377`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723377/Service+Exchange+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:09.335+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.

The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:

- Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.
- Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.
- Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.
- Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.
- Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extensions**

Enumeration

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:</p><ul><li>Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Enumeration</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722620 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00981: Service Exchange Manager dialog

- page_id: `136722620`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722620/Service+Exchange+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:26.018+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['service-exchange-manager-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1005008720

INLINE

1005008722

INLINE

1005008721

INLINE

The **Service Exchange Manager** dialog enables analysis and management of the Service Exchanges data. You can:

- Edit existing Service Exchanges by adding new Service Exchange Items
- Realize or ignore existing Service Exchanges on selected relationships
- Change the Service Exchange Item 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Review producing and consuming Service Function pairs and remove the ones that are not needed
- Review the list of diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange

To open a **Service Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selecta Service Association, Service Connector, Service Control Flow, Service Object Flow, or Service Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange_manager.png'] . The Service Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1005008719

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Service Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']

UAF

**Extension**

UML Operation

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721625 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00995: Service viewpoint

- page_id: `136721625`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721625/Service+viewpoint
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:32:05.879+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0
- labels: ['service-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824082325

824082327

824082326

The Service viewpoint is based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. It is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UAF12P2024x']. A service, within the Service viewpoint, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.

824082324

**Views**

A service, within the Service viewpoint, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="453cd4da-c781-4881-80ab-ef07bd44514f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">824082324</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00997: Services Connectivity

- page_id: `136722013`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722013/Services+Connectivity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:41:30.012+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919223678

919223680

919223679

**Description**

The Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain shows the interoperability among services. It specifies service interfaces, e.g. provided and required service operations, to ensure compatibility and reusability of services.

The purpose of the Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A Service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies the provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

**Implementation**

The Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Connectivity diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722041 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00998: Services Constraints

- page_id: `136722041`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722041/Services+Constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:23:51.349+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919305469

919305472

919305470

**Description**

The Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain shows the service policies that apply to the implementation of services. It specifies traditional textual service policies that are constraints on how services are implemented within resources. The addition of SysML parametrics provides a computational means of defining service policies across the enterprise or within a specific service configuration.

The purpose of the Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is to specify constraints that apply to providers and consumers of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of services, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. A Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain product specifies constraints against services to which service implementations must conform.

**Implementation**

The Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- Services Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Constraints table

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

919305467

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rule' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Contract' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Policy' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='']

It specifies traditional textual service policies that are constraints on how services are implemented within resources. The addition of SysML parametrics provides a computational means of defining service policies across the enterprise or within a specific service configuration.</p><p>The purpose of the Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is to specify constraints that apply to providers and consumers of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of services, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. A Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain product specifies constraints against services to which service implementations must conform.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Services Constraints table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Constraints table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Services Constraints Definition diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Constraints Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Services Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_constraints.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Services Constraints" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Services Constraints table</h6><p><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p>You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the <strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p>To select a constraint owner</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Go to <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong>, and in the <strong>Default Constraint Owners</strong> property group, choose a needed owner.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aad6f601-5461-4063-be33-42bf9a73998e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">919305467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Rule" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Contract" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Policy" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722108 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00999: Services Motivation

- page_id: `136722108`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722108/Services+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:58.840+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Services Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Services Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

**Description**

The Services Parameters (Sv-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of Services.

The intended usage of the Services Parameters (Sv-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

## PAGE 01001: Services Processes

- page_id: `136722048`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722048/Services+Processes
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:17:17.195+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

920305902

920305904

920305903

**Description**

The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain shows the behavior of a service in terms of the operational activities and functions it is expected to support. It provides detailed information regarding the allocation of service functions to service specifications, and data flows between service functions. The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain is the key behavioral specification for services.

**Implementation**

The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Processes Flow diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- Services Processes Flow diagram (BPD). It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. An Services Processes Flow (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Processes diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Processes Flow diagram

920305901

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='']

It provides detailed information regarding the allocation of service functions to service specifications, and data flows between service functions. The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain is the key behavioral specification for services.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Services Processes diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Processes diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Services Processes Flow diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Processes Flow diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Services Processes Flow diagram (BPD). The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities. An Services Processes Flow (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_processes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Services Processes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Services Processes diagram</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_processes_flow.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Services Processes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">An example of the Services Processes Flow diagram</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3ab754b-2cde-4e85-993c-0e4e84ba887f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">920305901</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Function Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Method" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Parameter" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ae645b3b-75d5-48ba-9c81-2fc4871ef96c" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722056 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01002: Services Roadmap

- page_id: `136722056`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722056/Services+Roadmap
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:16:31.835+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921650000

921650003

921650002

**Description**

The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain shows the service changes over time. It provides an overview of how service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services that evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain is represented by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] which is realized as a time based diagram.

921649999

It provides an overview of how service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.</p><p>In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services that evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.</p><p>The intended usage of the Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Development of incremental acquisition strategy.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain is represented by a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Services Roadmap diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Roadmap diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> which is realized as a time based diagram.<span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76378499-f971-4cea-8ba5-30c9d1112044"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">921649999</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Milestone Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Versioned Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Version Of Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Version Succession" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Whole Life Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Whole Life Configuration Kind" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bc1802df-2234-44f6-868a-4cc61460e342" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722064 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01003: Services Sequences

- page_id: `136722064`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722064/Services+Sequences
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:19:30.779+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919410764

919410767

919410766

**Description**

The Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain shows the behavior of a service specification in terms of expected time-ordered examination of the interactions between service roles. It specifies how service roles interact with each other, service providers and consumers, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions.

The purpose of the Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain is to specify how a service interacts with external agents and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. A Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services. Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

The Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Service Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] that is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Role' space='']

It specifies how service roles interact with each other, service providers and consumers, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions.</p><p>The purpose of the Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain is to specify how a service interacts with external agents and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. A Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services. Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain is represented by <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Service Sequences diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Sequences diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that is based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UML Sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Service Interaction Scenario" ac:alt="Service Interaction Scenario"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_interaction_scenario.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Services Sequences" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d6fc39e-4d27-4cc2-a847-2936640085e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">919410763</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Message" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Method" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Role" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c1546df5-56a2-451f-bbb0-50405803ed38" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722067 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01004: Services States

- page_id: `136722067`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722067/Services+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:57.935+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921781882

921781884

921781883

**Description**

The Services States (Sv-St) domain shows the behavior of a service specification in terms of states and events causing transitions between states. It specifies the possible states a service specification may have, and the possible transitions between those states.

The purpose of the Services States (Sv-St) domain is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. The Services States (Sv-St) domain is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

The Services States (Sv-St) domain is represented by a Services States diagram that is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services States diagram

921781881

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service State Description' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024x']

It specifies the possible states a service specification may have, and the possible transitions between those states.</p><p>The purpose of the Services States (Sv-St) domain is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. The Services States (Sv-St) domain is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services States (Sv-St) domain is represented by a Services States diagram that is based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UML State Machine diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_states.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Services States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Services States diagram </h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05249d80-0bda-4cd6-806f-bb755fc7453c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">921781881</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service State Description" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

The Services Structure (Sv-Sr) domain shows the the composition of services and how services are combined into a higher level service required to exhibit a capability or support an operational activity.

## PAGE 01006: Services Taxonomy

- page_id: `136722086`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722086/Services+Taxonomy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:42:10.473+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921953081

921953084

921953083

**Description**

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain shows the service types and required and provided service levels of these types.

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are services (i.e., service interfaces), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e., one Service is a special type of another.

**Implementation**

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] , which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024x'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Services Taxonomy diagram

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='']
