<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722098 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01007: Services Traceability

- page_id: `136722098`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722098/Services+Traceability
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:18:58.378+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922069205

922069207

922069206

**Description**

The Services Traceability (Sv-St) domain shows the traceability between operational activities and services that support them and depicts how services contribute to the achievement of a capability.

**Implementation**

The Services Traceability (Sv-St) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] . The intended usage of the Service Exhibits Traceability includes:
  - Tracing Capability requirements to Services .
  - Capability audit.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']. 
The intended usage of the Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix includes:
  - Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.
  - Tracing solution options to requirements.
  - Identification of overlaps or gaps.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] . The Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

922069204

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720780 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01008: Services viewpoint

- page_id: `136720780`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720780/Services+viewpoint
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:22:19.778+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['services-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394609712

394609714

394609713

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Services Viewpoint describes services and their interconnections providing or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Service Models associate service resources to the operational and capability requirements. These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

394609711

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe3fef66-fa3e-413c-90fb-99c497b3c658"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609712</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3f99e09-18a9-4aab-be9f-618b4506968b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609714</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="911042dd-8152-48cd-97d4-803d645a0bc6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609713</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>“The DoDAF-described Models within the Services Viewpoint describes services and their interconnections providing or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Service Models associate service resources to the operational and capability requirements. These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c152295-e2cc-45fa-b570-1afd899df865"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609711</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723150 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01009: ServicesBToSSync 1

- page_id: `136723150`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723150/ServicesBToSSync+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:56.731+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ServicesBToSSync[1]

**Description**

No compatible Service Interface. Thisvalidation rule checks whether Service Ports and Service Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Service Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Create Service Port - creates a new Service Port typed by a compatible Service Interface.
- Choose Compatible ServiceInterface - changes an existing Service Port type to a compatible Service Interface.
- Add Missing Flow Property - adds a new Flow Property to a Service Interface that types a Service Port.
- Reverse Direction of Service Port - changes the Service Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of a Service Interface that types a Service Port.

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**The Service Port*iPayment*is created using Behavior to Structure Synchronization solver.

- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ServicesBToSSync[2]

**Description**

No compatible ServiceConnector. The second validation rule checks whether a Service Connector exists between the two compatible Service Ports owned by the Asset Roles. The missing Service Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

**Create Service Connector** - creates a missing Service Connector between the two compatible Service Ports.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Creating a Service Connector that is missing in the Service Internal Connectivity Diagram.

## PAGE 01011: ServicesBToSSync 3

- page_id: `136723154`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723154/ServicesBToSSync+3
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-08T14:06:00.275+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ServicesBToSSync[3]

**Description**

No compatible Service Exchange realization. The third validation rule checks realized Service Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Service Exchanges are realized on Service Connectors that connect Service Ports typed by compatible Service Interface.Themissing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Realize Missing Service Exchange - r ealizes existing compatible Service Exchange found in the behavior model on the Service Connector.
- Open Service Exchange Manager - opens the Service Exchange Manager dialog.
- Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking - opens the Project Options dialog to turn on/off the Exchange realization checking.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realizing a missing Operational Exchange on the Connector in the Operational Internal Connectivity Diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ServicesBToSSync[3]</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">No compatible Service Exchange realization. The third validation rule checks realized Service Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Service Exchanges are realized on Service Connectors that connect Service Ports typed by compatible Service Interface.<span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>missing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Diagram<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Realize Missing Service Exchange</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">r</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ealizes existing compatible Service Exchange found in the behavior model on the Service Connector.</span></li><li><strong>Open Service Exchange Manager</strong> - opens the <strong>Service Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog.</li><li><strong>Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">opens the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Project Options</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>dialog to turn on/off the Exchange realization checking. </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_behavior_to_structure_services_exchanges_validation_rule_process.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5d5b7f9e-ce01-4e9c-a49f-30f4811c5bec">Realizing a missing Operational Exchange on the Connector in the Operational Internal Connectivity Diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722834 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01012: Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets

- page_id: `136722834`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722834/Setting+up+Automatic+Population+of+Measurement+Sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:35.903+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['set-up-automatic-population-of-measurement-sets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can set up the automatic measurementSet population each time creating the Instance of the associated type. 
 
 To set up the automatic Measurement Set population

1. Create a Measurement Set.
2. Add measurements for the created Measurement Set.
3. Apply « DoDAF Properties» stereotype to the created Measurement Set.
4. Open the Measurement Set Specification window and make sure the Expert properties mode is selected.
5. In the Tags property group, select the appliesFor and click the Create Value button.
6. Select the Exchange Element stereotype as the appliesFor tag value. [ATTACHMENT filename='exchange_element_as_the appliedFor_tag_value.png']
7. Save and reload the project.

You can see and edit your custom measurements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to see your measurements in a separate group within Exchange Element specification, add group name to the Measurement Set documentation field.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721250 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01014: SOV-3 Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136721250`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721250/SOV-3+Working+with+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:55.626+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-3 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SOV-3 Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-4a Service Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

374424572

374424575

374424574

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specification. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

374424571

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5352cfdc-efd8-4a4e-9386-399ed9cb8bc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">374424571</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721684 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01016: Sr Service Roadmap

- page_id: `136721684`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721684/Sr+Service+Roadmap
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:24:04.243+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1974596998

1974597000

1974596999

**Description**

The Sr Services Roadmap domain shows the service changes over time. It provides an overview of how a service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Sr Services Roadmap domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the Sr Services Roadmap domain includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Sr Services Roadmap domain is represented by a Services Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

1974596997

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Milestone Dependency' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Versioned Element' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Of Configuration' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Succession' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration Kind' space='']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="367ab366-ea0b-4d39-8a75-ce13652204b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1974596998</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f17ef9c-0f09-4543-ab06-81691905ea75"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1974597000</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="139df0e7-7eaa-4bf2-aec2-c7053b95f96d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1974596999</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Sr Services Roadmap domain shows the service changes over time. It provides an overview of how a service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Sr Services Roadmap domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.</p><p>In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.</p><p>The intended usage of the Sr Services Roadmap domain includes:</p><ul><li>Development of incremental acquisition strategy.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Sr Services Roadmap domain is represented by a Services Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram. </p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bbbf1c0-0b44-4a22-96a8-244a2ccd5f95"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1974596997</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Milestone Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Versioned Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Version Of Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Version Succession" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Whole Life Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Whole Life Configuration Kind" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

**Description**

The Standards Parameters (Sd-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Standards Parameters (Sd-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722441 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01022: Standards Roadmap

- page_id: `136722441`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722441/Standards+Roadmap
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:21:42.139+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922198773

922198775

922198774

**Description**

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain shows the expected changes in technology-related standards and conventions, operational standards, or business standards and conventions. It defines the underlying current and expected standards. Expected standards are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology, and expected improvements/trends.

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain complements and expands on the Standards Taxonomy and Standards Traceability views and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is implemented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Roadmap table' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain complements and expands on the Standards Taxonomy and Standards Traceability views and should be used when more than one emerging standard **Description**

The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain shows technical and non-technical standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture. It shows the taxonomy of types of technical, operational, and business standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture.

The intended usage of the Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] diagram.

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c2c4d4c-6c7d-4482-a715-946f460366a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922668140</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33abd6e9-f1a3-471a-80df-68e7eaaa7e05"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922668142</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ab1cc74-3f20-4cfd-8097-a8a73d26d941"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922668141</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain shows technical and non-technical standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture. It shows the taxonomy of types of technical, operational, and business standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture.</p><p>The intended usage of the Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Application of standards (informing project strategy).</li><li>Standards compliance.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Standards Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Standards Taxonomy]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Standards Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Standards Taxonomy diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b07eab8-78c8-44a9-a8fe-d290b07c31ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922668139</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Organization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Protocol" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Protocol Layer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Protocol Stack" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF Element" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722474 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01025: Standards Traceability

- page_id: `136722474`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722474/Standards+Traceability
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:41:09.248+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922795287

922795290

922795289

**Description**

The Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain shows the traceability between capabilities and projects that deliver them. It depicts the mapping of projects to capabilities and thus identifies the transformation of a capability(ies) into a purposeful implementation via projects.

The intended usage of the Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

The Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Traceability table' space='UAF12P2024x']Standards Traceability table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Standards Traceability table

922795286

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Element' space='']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721087 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01026: Standards viewpoint

- page_id: `136721087`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721087/Standards+viewpoint
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:16:19.429+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['standards-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408621248

408621250

408621249

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Standards Viewpoint is the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of parts or elements of the Architectural Description. These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

408621246

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e47f7ae5-e1b0-4eba-8693-154d4af87ad0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408621246</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="730c63a7-72f7-471c-859d-28a9d0bf86db" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721088 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01028: StdV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `136721088`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721088/StdV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:49.727+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-1-standards-profile', 'stdv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408664615

408664617

408664616

**Description**

The StdV-1 defines the technical, operational, and business standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture being described. As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the DoDAF V2.0 StdV-1 also documents the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context. The DISR is an architecture resource for technical standards that can be used in the generation of the StdV-1 and StdV-2 Standards Forecast.

In most cases, building a Standards Profile consists of identifying and listing the applicable portions of existing and emerging documentation. A StdV-1 should identify both existing guidelines, as well as any areas lacking guidance. As with other models, each profile is assigned a specific timescale (e.g., “As-Is”, “To-Be”, or transitional). Linking the profile to a defined timescale enables the profile to consider both emerging technologies and any current technical standards that are expected to be updated or become obsolete. If more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to an architecture, then a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x'] should be completed as well as a StdV-1.

The intended usage of the StdV-1 includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

StdV-1 can be represented by a StdV-1 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Standards cited are referenced as relationships to the systems, services, system functions, service functions, system data, service data, hardware/software items or communication protocols, where applicable, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024x'].

408664614

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol Stack' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-1 table' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the DoDAF V2.0 StdV-1 also documents the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context. The DISR is an architecture resource for technical standards that can be used in the generation of the StdV-1 and StdV-2 Standards Forecast.</p><p>In most cases, building a Standards Profile consists of identifying and listing the applicable portions of existing and emerging documentation. A StdV-1 should identify both existing guidelines, as well as any areas lacking guidance. Linking the profile to a defined timescale enables the profile to consider both emerging technologies and any current technical standards that are expected to be updated or become obsolete. If more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to an architecture, then a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link> should be <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721092 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01029: StdV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `136721092`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721092/StdV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:50.127+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-2-standards-forecast', 'stdv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408737149

408737151

408737150

**Description**

The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024x'] model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024x'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x'] models.

A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024x'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024x']) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x'].

408737148

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol Stack' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dab08df-45ed-463f-9891-33ad7dc25deb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737149</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="508d5c6e-be40-4292-b0a8-f37df7f0298f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737151</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a3450f7-473b-46f2-95f8-cea894e70d93"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737150</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="StdV-1 Standards Profile" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StdV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9 Systems Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9 Services Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> models.</p><p>A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p>The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" ac:alt="StdV-2 Standards Forecast"><ri:attachment ri:filename="TV-2_StdV-2.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>,<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /></ac:link>) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9 Systems Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9 Services Technology & Skills Forecast models]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="acf44592-71cd-48ac-a7fd-0d8fc1c99390"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737148</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Standard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Protocol" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Forecast" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Protocol Stack" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating StdV-2 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721824 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01030: Strategic Connectivity

- page_id: `136721824`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721824/Strategic+Connectivity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:28:59.776+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922916381

922916383

922916382

**Description**

The Strategic Connectivity (St-Cn) diagram depicts relationships between capabilities in which one capability cannot succeed without some form of assistance from another capability. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.

The intended usage of the Strategic Connectivity (St-Cn) includes:

- Identification of capability dependencies.
- Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).

**Implementation**

The Strategic Connectivity (St-Cn) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Connectivity matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Connectivity matrix

## PAGE 01032: Strategic Constraints

- page_id: `136721837`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721837/Strategic+Constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:32:12.142+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1102244620

1102244623

1102244622

**Description**

The Strategic Constraints (St-Ct) details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Constraints (St-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] diagram based on a UML Class diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a Strategic Constraint Definition diagram.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

1102244619

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set' space='']

1959035768

1959035779

1959035778

**Description**

The **Strategic Information** (St-If) view addresses information that can be considered to be an enterprise strategic asset that can influence the achievement of enterprise goals. It identifies and defines those strategic information elements and their relationships that are applicable to the architecture.

**Implementation**

The **Strategic Information** (St-If) is represented by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Information diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

1959035767

It identifies and defines those strategic information elements and their relationships that are applicable to the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The <strong>Strategic Information</strong> (St-If) is represented by the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Information diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Information diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c13da77-102e-48f3-9e93-df769a9e1076"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1959035767</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Information diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01036: Strategic Motivation

- page_id: `136721901`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721901/Strategic+Motivation

- labels: []

1909691137

1909691158

1909691147

**Description**

The **Strategic Motivation** (St-Mv) view identifies and defines the drivers, challenges, and opportunities that are applicable to the architecture. It defines the desired outcomes, goals, and objectives that are motivated by the drivers, and the opportunities that enable the goals and objectives.

**Implementation**

The **Strategic Motivation** (St-Mv) is represented by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Motivation diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Related procedures**

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="273e3161-3aaf-43fc-abad-dac36d1394e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1909691137</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7f2435a-4273-48fb-96fa-6d2e3d2ca24b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1909691158</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36929bdd-9d9c-4429-818a-30ccef19393a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1909691147</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Strategic Motivation</strong> (St-Mv) view identifies and defines the drivers, challenges, and opportunities that are applicable to the architecture. It defines the desired outcomes, goals, and objectives that are motivated by the drivers, and the opportunities that enable the goals and objectives.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The <strong>Strategic Motivation</strong> (St-Mv) is represented by the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Motivation diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Motivation diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3a2b18b-72f8-400a-92f1-05b2352be90f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1909691136</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Motivation diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

**Description**

The Strategic Parameters (St-Pm) view depicts the measures (metrics) of strategic elements.

The intended usage of the Strategic Parameters (St-Pm) view includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Measurements (St-Pm) domain is represented by:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721848 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01040: Strategic Roadmap

- page_id: `136721848`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721848/Strategic+Roadmap
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:42:54.766+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922995112

922995115

922995114

**Description**

The Strategic Roadmap (St-Rm) view represents the fulfillment of capability requirements and planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time.

The **Strategic Deployment** table shows the planned capability deployment for an actual resource and the responsible actual organization.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Roadmap (St-Rm) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] . This diagram addresses the deployment of capabilities to actual organizations over time. This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The Strategic Deployment diagram can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding. The intended usage of the Strategic Deployment diagram includes:
  - Fielding planning.
  - Capability integration planning.
  - Capability options analysis.
  - Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
  - Identification of deployment level shortfalls.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Phasing diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] . That diagram is used to plan achievement of capabilities at different points in time or during specific periods of time. The Strategic Phasing diagram supports the capability audit processes and similar processes used across the different COIs by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. This diagram indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries). The intended usage of the Strategic Phasing diagram includes:
  - Capability planning (capability phasing).
  - Capability integration planning.
  - Capability gap analysis.
- Strategic Deployment Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx).

**Samples**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Deployment diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Phasing diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart

1094426394

1094426396

1094426395

**Description**

The Strategic States (St-St) view shows the relationships between capability(ies) and desired effect(s) that implementation(s) of capability(ies) should achieve. It defines the different measurements required to define what the architecture is meant to achieve in quantitative measures.

**Implementation**

The Strategic States (St-St) view is represented by the Strategic States diagram based on a UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a Strategic States diagram.

ac:name="id">1094426395</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Strategic States (St-St) view shows the relationships between capability(ies) and desired effect(s) that implementation(s) of capability(ies) should achieve. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>It defines the different measurements required to define what the architecture is meant to achieve in quantitative measures.</span></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Strategic States (St-St) view is represented by the Strategic States diagram based on a UML Class diagram.</span></p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Strategic_states_diagram_sample.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Strategic States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of a Strategic States diagram.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ## PAGE 01042: Strategic Structure

- page_id: `136721865`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721865/Strategic+Structure
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:16:38.215+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923196921

923196923

923196922

**Description**

The Strategic Structure (St-Sr) shows the composition of capabilities and how capabilities are decomposed from higher level ones as needed. This view addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of capabilities. The purpose of a Strategic Structure (St-Sr) view is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Structure (St-Sr) view is represented by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Structure diagram

923196920

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721880 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01043: Strategic Taxonomy

- page_id: `136721880`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721880/Strategic+Taxonomy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:31:20.472+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923322042

923322045

923322044

**Description**

The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) describes the relationships between individual capabilities including composition, association, and generalization.

The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.

The intended usage of the Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view includes:

- Identification of capability requirements.
- Capability planning (capability taxonomy).
- Codifying required capability elements.
- Capability audit.
- Capability gap analysis.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of user requirements.
- Providing reference capabilities for architectures.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024x'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Taxonomy table

923322041

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721893 space=UAF12P2024x version=6 -->
## PAGE 01044: Strategic Traceability

- page_id: `136721893`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721893/Strategic+Traceability
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2024-04-11T13:08:46.788+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Strategic Traceability (St-Tr) describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the phasing constructs and association with relevant challenges and drivers. With a help of the Strategic Traceability (St-Tr) view specification, you can identify howthe required capabilities and the actual strategic phases that those actual strategic phases support are mapped.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Traceability (St-Tr) view specification is represented by an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space=''] which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Rows of this matrix are Actual Strategic Phases and the Columns are Capabilities.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping matrix

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Phases' space='']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723400 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01046: Structural Part

- page_id: `136723400`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723400/Structural+Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:10.091+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the structure of the Enterprise Phase.

**Architecture Framework**

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF

**Extension**

UML Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the structure of the Enterprise Phase. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle2">DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Property</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721818 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01051: Summary and Overview

- page_id: `136721818`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721818/Summary+and+Overview
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:28:19.031+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923507957

923507959

923507958

**Description**

The Summary & Overview viewpoint is for modeling the executive-level summary information in a consistent form. It provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions.

The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain depicts the measurements. The intended usage of The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Summary & Overview domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Summary and Overview diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] . It shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide. Upon completion of architecture, it provides a summary of findings and any conducted analysis.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Date and Time Summary Table' space='UAF12P2024x'] depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.
- Reports (.docx): - Summary and Overview. - All View.

The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx)

****

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Summary and Overview diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723487 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01053: Supports relationship between Operational Activities and Services in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `136723487`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723487/Supports+relationship+between+Operational+Activities+and+Services+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:16.902+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the migration to UAF, the Supports relationships between Operational Activities and Services are created. The relationships are displayed in the following matrices:

UAF: Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

DoDAF: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

NAF: NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping

NAF4: S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After the migration to UAF, the Supports relationships between Operational Activities and Services are created. The relationships are displayed in the following matrices:</p><p>UAF: Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix</p><p>DoDAF: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix</p><p>NAF: NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping</p><p>NAF4: S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720920 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01054: SV-1 Systems Interface Description

- page_id: `136720920`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720920/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:45.410+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-description', 'sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404622161

404622164

404622162

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of systems. For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of performers- Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x']. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key resource flows to non-technical stakeholders.

A system resource flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all system resource flows between systems that are of interest. Note that resource flows between systems may be further specified in detail in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'].

Sub-system assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those resources. In many cases, an Operational Activity and Locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- System resource flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the resource flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performersoriginally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performerto another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x']. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']).

The system Functions performed by the performersare specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024x'] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x']specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

404622159

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Architecture' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Technology' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interface' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Connector' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Geo Political Extent Type' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024x']

For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performers </span>- Organizations and Personnel Types.</p><p>The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /></ac:link>. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key resource flows to non-technical stakeholders.</p><p>A system resource flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all system resource flows between systems that are of interest. Note that resource flows between systems may be further specified in detail in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Sub-system assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those resources. In many cases, an Operational Activity and Locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of system concepts.</li><li>Definition of system options.</li><li>System resource flow requirements capture.</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>System integration management.</li><li>Operational planning (capability and performer definition).</li></ul><p>The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:</p><ul><li>Describe the resource flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.</li><li>Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ac:alt="SV-1 Systems Interface Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performers </span>originally specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performer </span>to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p>The system Functions performed by the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performers </span>are specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4 System Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and
