<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720914 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01079: SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `136720914`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720914/SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:45.041+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10c-services-event-trace-description', 'svcv-10c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397642427

397642429

397642428

**Description**

The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']), Resource Flows (data, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x']) and information elements (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024x']) modeled in other models.

397642426

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af83de02-9f7b-4d06-a4ff-36f48f22a3a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397642427</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90689398-4224-4d94-9c58-cf8111ad5ec6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397642429</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da94282c-9854-4736-bb2d-fc1a7327d555"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397642428</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" ac:alt="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10c.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>), Resource Flows (data, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span>) and information elements (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7d22c24-6109-4d78-8000-db93634c03a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397642426</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Message" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Method" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720918 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01080: SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `136720918`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720918/SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:45.269+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720785 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01081: SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `136720785`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720785/SvcV-2+Services+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.239+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-2-services-resource-flow-description', 'svcv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395159322

395159324

395159323

**Description**

A SvcV-2 specifies the Resource Flows between Service Specificationsand may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SvcV-2 DoDAF-described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between Service Specifications. This may be an existing connection or a specification of a connection that is to be made for a future connection.

The intended usage of the SvcV-2 includes:

- Resource Flow specification.

**Implementation**

SvcV-2 can be represented using:

- SvcV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SvcV-2 Internal Block diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SvcV-2 diagram needs be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024x']StdV-1 Standards Profile.

395159321

**Related elements**

This may be an existing connection or a specification of a connection that is to be made for a future connection.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-2 includes:</p><ul><li>Resource Flow specification.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-2 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SvcV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>SvcV-2 Internal Block <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720790 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01082: SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix

- page_id: `136720790`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720790/SvcV-3a+Systems-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.385+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3a-systems-services-matrix', 'svcv-3a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395570067

395570069

395570068

**Description**

A SvcV-3a enables a quick overview of all the system-to-service resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] models. The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the system and services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. This model can be useful in support existing systems that are transitioning to provide services. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SvcV-3a can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of system-to-service interactions in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3a includes:

- Summarizing system and service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3a can be represented using a SvcV-3a diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the systemand servicesinteractions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024x']. Each Resource Exchangelisted in the SvcV-3a table should be traceable to Operational Exchange listed in the corresponding [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']. Each Resource Exchangemay relate to a known Function (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x']) that produces or consumes it. DataElements as well as the other type of Resource ExchangeItems are exchanged by Service Providers and Service Requesters represented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']SV-6 table.

395570066

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720793 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01083: SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix

- page_id: `136720793`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720793/SvcV-3b+Services-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.467+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3b-services-services-matrix', 'svcv-3b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396077917

396077919

396077918

**Description**

A SvcV-3b enables a quick overview of all the services resource interactions specified in one or more SvcV-1 Services Context Description models. The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.

The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:

- Summarizing service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10a Services Rules Model' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description' space='UAF12P2024x'], implemented as orchestrations of services.

396077916

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-3b matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']

The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p><p>The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing service resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.</p><p>This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV- 10a Services Rules Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" /></ac:link>, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2284c6e9-45c1-4164-ab73-42370aceb193"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396077916</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-3b matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720796 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01084: SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description

- page_id: `136720796`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720796/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.541+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-4-services-functionality-description', 'svcv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396204750

396204752

396204751

**Description**

The SvcV-4 DoDAF-described Model addresses human and service functionality.

The primary purpose of SvcV-4 is to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the service functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Services Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of service functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between service functions.

The SvcV-4 is the Services Viewpoint counterpart to the OV-5b Operational Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SvcV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional service requirements.
- Functional decomposition of Services.
- Relate human and service functions.

**Implementation**

SvcV-4 can be represented using:

- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4.png']
- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram which is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4_flow.png']

The SvcV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x']).

396204748

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

/></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p>The SvcV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link> (in the same way that <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is the behavioral counterpart to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /></ac:link>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c38c5c32-88d4-4d24-89e0-28f0af597818"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396204748</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720828 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01085: SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `136720828`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720828/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.698+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720829 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01086: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `136720829`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720829/SvcV-5+Services+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.735+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-5-operational-activity-to-services-traceability-matrix', 'svcv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396601259

396601261

396601260

**Description**

The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:

- Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SvcV-5 can be represented using:

- The SvcV-5 diagram which is non-editable Dependency Matrix.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] . The Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x']. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.

396601258

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supports' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-5 matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f38cfce8-37f1-4852-90a1-54414c4da8f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396601259</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fb803c1-c08b-4af8-90df-7fe3f87b439c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396601261</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ada1b18-74bf-4c2f-b7c3-108f22ddcd82"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396601260</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>The SvcV-5 diagram which is non-editable Dependency Matrix.</li><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The<span> </span></span>Services to Service Contracts Mapping<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</span></li></ul><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df74b75d-85fb-41b5-a96f-90c468376485"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396601258</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Supports" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-5 matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720836 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01087: SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `136720836`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720836/SvcV-6+Role-based+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.960+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix** (SvcV-6) specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between service roles.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click **+** button.In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Interactions, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix</strong> (SvcV-6) specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between service roles.</p><h3>Adding existing Service Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Other cells are read-only.</span></li><li>To create and modify the Service Interactions, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720835 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01088: SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `136720835`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720835/SvcV-6+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.916+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix** addresses service exchanges between services.

You can add any available kind (such as Information Exchange, Materiel Exchange, Organizational Exchange, Energy Exchange) of Service Exchanges in this table.

##### Adding new Service Exchanges

To add new Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Services Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Service Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Service Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Service Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Service Exchanges are added to the table.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) diagram.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Service Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchange data.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024x'] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
4. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix</strong> addresses service exchanges between services.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f572216b-74e7-460e-bb6a-59e6ea7a4ac5"><ac:rich-text-body>You can add any available kind (such as Information Exchange, Materiel Exchange, Organizational Exchange, Energy Exchange) of Service Exchanges in this table.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Service Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Services Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Service Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Service Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Service Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Service Exchanges by clicking the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Service Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are editable in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Other cells are read-only.</span></li><li>To <span>create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use</span> Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) diagram.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchange data.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding Service Functions</h3><p>To add Service Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Service Functions</strong> button. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Producing and Consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[choose the producing and consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720837 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01089: SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix

- page_id: `136720837`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720837/SvcV-7+Services+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:44.010+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-7-services-measures-matrix', 'svcv-7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396906475

396906478

396906477

**Description**

The SvcV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Services Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in an SvcV-1 Services Context Description by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description.

In addition, this model is useful in support of the net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. Service measures for Service Level Agreements for each service and may include a number of service consumers, service usage by consumers, and the minimum, average and maximum response times, allowed downtime, etc. Measures of interest for a Chief Information Office or Program manager may include measures that assess service reuse, process efficiency, and business agility.

The intended usage of the SvcV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-7 can be represented using:

- SvcV-7 Typical measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_typical.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- SvcV-7 Actual measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_actual.png']
- SvcV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report

396906474

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating an Actual Measurements table from a Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720842 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01090: SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description

- page_id: `136720842`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720842/SvcV-8+Services+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:44.124+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-8-services-evolution-description', 'svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397044898

397044900

397044899

**Description**

The SvcV-8 presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the SvcV-8 includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The SvcV-8 view can be represented using a SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

397044897

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720902 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01091: SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `136720902`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720902/SvcV-9+Services+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:44.435+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-9-services-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'svcv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397238836

397238838

397238837

**Description**

The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.

The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.

The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x'] into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.

397238834

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024x']

Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.</p><p>The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:</p><ul><li>Emerging capabilities.</li><li>Industry trends.</li><li>Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.</li><li>Current and possible future skills.</li></ul><p>In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting technology readiness against time.</li><li>HR Trends Analysis.</li><li>Recruitment Planning.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li><li>Input to options analysis.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.</p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" /></ac:link> can support). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720396 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01092: Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives

- page_id: `136720396`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720396/Switching+between+projects+with+different+AF+related+perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:33.789+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A particular behavior is applied when switching between projects with different Architecture Framework (AF) related perspectives.When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a message is displayed.You can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective in that message.

In the following table, see the cases of the actions required when switching between projects A and B.

| Actions of Project A | Project A perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project A | Actions of Project B | Project B perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project B | Required actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Open Project A | UAF | - | Open project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Open project A | UAF | - | Open project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Create Project A | UAF | MODAF | Create project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Open Project A | UAF | MODAF | Open project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | UAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the UAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to UAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | - | Switch to opened project B | NAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the NAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to NAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | NAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the NAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to NAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2(the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | MODAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the MODAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to MODAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2 (the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |

##### Examples of messages

Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.

**Case #1.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case #2.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723515 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01093: SysML Compliance mode changes

- page_id: `136723515`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723515/SysML+Compliance+mode+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:19.263+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.

The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype and SysML PartProperty stereotype):

- Capability Property
- Concept Role
- Known Resource
- Operational Role
- Problem Domain
- Resource Role
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.</p><p>The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype  and SysML PartProperty stereotype):</p><ul><li>Capability Property</li><li>Concept Role</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Role</li><li>Problem Domain</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723495 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01094: SysML Compliance mode changes in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `136723495`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723495/SysML+Compliance+mode+changes+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:17.827+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.

The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype and SysML PartProperty stereotype):

- Capability Property
- Concept Role
- Known Resource
- Operational Role
- Problem Domain
- Resource Role
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.</p><p>The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype  and SysML PartProperty stereotype):</p><ul><li>Capability Property</li><li>Concept Role</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Role</li><li>Problem Domain</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723406 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01095: System

- page_id: `136723406`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723406/System
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:10.320+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An integrated set of elements, subsystems, or assemblies that accomplish a defined objective. These elements include products (hardware, software, firmware), processes, people, information, techniques, facilities, services, and other support elements.

**Architecture** **Framework**

DoDAF, UAF

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p>An integrated set of elements, subsystems, or assemblies that accomplish a defined objective. These elements include products (hardware, software, firmware), processes, people, information, techniques, facilities, services, and other support elements.</p><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span class="fontstyle0">DoDAF, UAF</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Class</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720919 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01096: Systems viewpoint

- page_id: `136720919`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720919/Systems+viewpoint
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:30:11.533+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['systems-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404545554

404545557

404545555

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Systems Viewpoint describes systems and interconnections providing for, or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Systems Models associate systems resources to the operational and capability requirements. These systems resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The Systems DoDAF-described Models are available for support of legacy systems. As architectures are updated, they should transition from Systems to Services and utilize the models within the Services Viewpoint.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo**[***UPDM: Systems viewpoint*.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qvL33ckmJJY)

Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.

404545552

**Views**

**Description**

A sub type of Resource Artifact that indicates a technology domain, i.e. nuclear, mechanical, electronic, mobile telephony etc.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Class

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723408 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01098: Temporal Part

- page_id: `136723408`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723408/Temporal+Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:10.399+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the temporal structure of the Enterprise Phase.

**Architecture Framework**

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF

**Extension**

UML Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the temporal structure of the Enterprise Phase.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle2">DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Property</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722625 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01099: Time Periods dialog

- page_id: `136722625`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722625/Time+Periods+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:23:32.128+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['time-periods-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Button Visibility

1014964889

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Button Visibility

1014964891

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Button Visibility

1014964890

##### Time Periods

In the UPDM plugin, time periods are used to specify resources for the architecture against a timeline indicating when they are expected to be in use. Time periods can be defined when the subjects, or resources, of the forecast are already added in the table.As shown below, the**Technology area**column must be filled in with resources**before**adding a specific time period.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Time Periods Dialog

The **Time Periods** dialog is essential when defining time periods for the **forecast of the resources**.

The **Time Periods** dialog consists of three areas:

- **Toolbar** - This area allows you to control how properties in the dialog are displayed: Categorized view, Alphabetical view, Expand all, Collapse all.
- **Properties** - Provides a list of properties that are available to specify.
- Properties value cells - The fields where you can set values for each property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Time Periods dialog

Due to the abundance of button in the dialog, the instructions are broken down into two parts: General instructions and Specific instructions. General instructions will explain how to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']the Time Periods dialog and how to approach the main part of the Time Periods dialog: the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']. Specific instructions will explain how tospecify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024x'] in the dialog.

openTo open the **Time Periods** dialog

- Open the **Time Periods** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the table toolbar. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Button Visibility

Button Visibility

The button "Time Period" is visible **only** in the **Forecast** diagram or any diagram containing a forecast.

##### Specifying Timelines

When specifying timelines, you can either [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024x'] a pre-existing timeline or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024x'] a new one.

timelineTo specify the **Timeline**

There are two things you can do here:

- select Select a pre-existing Timeline.
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='alt+down.png'] , or Alt+Down for a drop-down menu to make your selection. [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_timeline.png']
- newtimeline Create a new Timeline.
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] for a **Select Timeline** dialog.
  2. Click Creation Mode. [ATTACHMENT filename='turned_on_creation_mode.png']
  3. Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline.
  4. Click Create. (Shortcut: Right-click and click Create. ) The new Timeline's specification window opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_timeline_button.png']
  5. Type the name of the new Timeline. Click Close. [ATTACHMENT filename='Specifying_name_of_new_timeline.png']
  6. Click OK.

1014964888

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Technology Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024x']

579277482

**Sample Model:**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **DoDAF Sample**. To open this sample, do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='DoDAF Sample.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\UPDM\DoDAF Sample.mdzip

Click </span><strong>Close.</strong><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Specification window of newly created Timeline with Name property specified as Software Time Line." ac:alt="The Specification window of newly created Timeline with Name property specified as Software Time Line."><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specifying_name_of_new_timeline.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK. </strong></li></ol></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e45e65c0-15f0-4a24-8dd2-215837471655"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1014964888</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating P1 Technology Forecast table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating A8 Standards Forecast table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating StdV-2 table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-9 table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating SV-9 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4094778-c6d2-419d-a9b2-5a6066673dca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">579277482</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample Model:</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>DoDAF Sample</strong>. To open this sample, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="DoDAF Sample.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDMP183" ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\UPDM\DoDAF Sample.mdzip</em></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
