<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722803 space=UAF12P2024x version=5 -->
## PAGE 01101: Traceability relation maps

- page_id: `136722803`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722803/Traceability+relation+maps
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2024-03-19T12:45:02.437+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing traceability and impact analysis
- labels: ['traceability-relation-maps', 'capability-structural-map', 'capability-behavioral-map', 'implementation-map', 'operational-activity-implementation-map', 'operational-activity-map', 'organization-structure-map', 'system-resource-map', 'variant-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model.

The UAF Plugin presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope.

You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.

The predefined relation maps are the following:

##### Capability Behavioral Map

The**Capability Behavioral Map**depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Activities (Operational Activity, Standard Operational Activity), related Functions (Function, Project Activity, Security Process), and related Service Functions. The Capability Behavioral Map also shows the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Capability Structural Map

The**Capability Structural Map**depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Agents (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture), Resource Performers (Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements), Services, Actual Resource, and Actual Service. The Capability Structural Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Implementation Map

The**Implementation Map**depicts the implementation relationships of an Operational Performer. This predefined map includes the Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture, Operational Interface, Operational Role, Operational Information), and the Resource Elements (Resource Performer, Resource Role, Resource Interface, Resource Information) that have been indicated as implementing that operational element. The Implementation Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Operational Activity Decomposition Map

The**Operational Activity Decomposition Map**depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.

##### Operational Activity Implementation Map

The**Operational Activity Implementation Map**depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.

##### **Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map**

The**Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map**depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Performer. This predefined map includes:

- Performs Operational Activity
- Exhibits Capability
- Context (Operational Roles, where Operational Performer is used as Context)
- Inputs and Outputs (Operational Performers connected using Operational Exchanges)
- Operational Exchanges

##### **Operational Role Impact Analysis Map**

The**Operational Role Impact Analysis Map**depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Role. This predefined map includes:

- Context (Operational Performer that is the context of the Operational Role)
- Inputs and Outputs (Operational Roles connected using Operational Connectors)
- Performs In Context (Operational Activities performed by the Operational Role)
- Performs (by Type) (Operational Activities performed by the Type of the Operational Role)
- Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Operational Role)

##### Organization Structure Map

The**Organization Structure Map**depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes:

- Actual Organization itself
- Actual Posts
- Actual Persons

The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.

##### **Resource Performer Map**

The**Resource Performer Map**depicts the structural decomposition of a Resource Performer including Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements.

##### **Resource Role Impact Analysis Map**

The**Resource Role Impact Analysis Map**depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Resource Role. This predefined map includes:

- Context (Resource which is used as the context of the Resource Role)
- Inputs and Outputs (Resource Roles connected using Resource Connectors)
- Performs In Context (Functions performed by the Resource Role)
- Performs (by Type) (Function Actions performed by the Type of the Resource Role)
- Implements (by Type) (Operational Performers implemented by the Type of the Resource Role)
- Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Resource Role)

##### Strategic Driver Map

**The Strategic Driver Map (St-Mv)**depicts the relationships of a Driver.

This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.

The Context of the relation map can be either a Driver or a Package. The Relationship Criteria include Owned Element (if the Context Element is Package), Motivated By, Phases, Is Composed of Enterprise Goals, and Is Composed of Enterprise Objectives. The Element Type includes Driver, Enterprise Goal, Enterprise Objective, and Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Variant Map

The**Variant Map**depicts the specializations of a Resource Performer, including Capability Configuration, Software, and other elements. Specializations of a Resource Performer are modeled using the Generalization relationship.

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model.</p><p>The UAF Plugin presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a815edba-6ddc-4108-9f70-02174da37c82"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="confluence-link">The predefined relation maps are the following:</p><p class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0983454-d2b0-4d94-bfa5-ca17ef10aa9c" /></p><h3>Capability Behavioral Map</h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Capability Behavioral Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Activities (Operational Activity, Standard Operational Activity), related Functions (Function, Project Activity, Security Process), and related Service Functions. The Capability Behavioral Map also shows the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><h3>Capability Structural Map</h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Capability Structural Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Agents (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture), Resource Performers (Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements), Services, Actual Resource, and Actual Service. The Capability Structural Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><h3>Implementation Map</h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Implementation Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the implementation relationships of an Operational Performer. This predefined map includes the Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture, Operational Interface, Operational Role, Operational Information), and the Resource Elements (Resource Performer, Resource Role, Resource Interface, Resource Information) that have been indicated as implementing that operational element. The Implementation Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Creating Implementation Map" ac:alt="Creating Implementation Map"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_map.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><span>Operational Activity Decomposition Map</span></h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Operational Activity Decomposition Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity. </p><h3>Operational Activity Implementation Map</h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Operational Activity Implementation Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements. </p><h3><strong>Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</strong></h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Performer. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Performs Operational Activity</li><li>Exhibits Capability</li><li>Context (Operational Roles, where Operational Performer is used as Context)</li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Operational Performers connected using Operational Exchanges)</li><li>Operational Exchanges</li></ul><h3><strong>Operational Role Impact Analysis Map        </strong></h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Operational Role Impact Analysis Map<span> </span></strong>depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Role. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Context (Operational Performer that is the context of the Operational Role)  </li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Operational Roles connected using Operational Connectors)</li><li>Performs In Context (Operational Activities performed by the Operational Role)</li><li>Performs (by Type) (Operational Activities performed by the Type of the Operational Role)</li><li>Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Operational Role)</li></ul><h3>Organization Structure Map</h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Organization Structure Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Actual Organization itself</li><li>Actual Posts</li><li>Actual Persons</li></ul><p>The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.</p><h3><strong>Resource Performer Map</strong></h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Resource Performer Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the structural decomposition of a Resource Performer including Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements.</p><h3><strong>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</strong></h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Resource Role. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Context (Resource which is used as the context of the Resource Role)</li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Resource Roles connected using Resource Connectors)</li><li>Performs In Context (Functions performed by the Resource Role)</li><li>Performs (by Type) (Function Actions performed by the Type of the Resource Role)</li><li>Implements (by Type) (Operational Performers implemented by the Type of the Resource Role)</li><li>Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Resource Role) </li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Strategic Driver Map</span></h3><p><strong>The Strategic Driver Map (St-Mv)<span> </span></strong>depicts the relationships of a Driver.</p><p>This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.   </p><p>The Context of the relation map can be either a Driver or a Package. The Relationship Criteria include Owned Element (if the Context Element is Package), Motivated By, Phases, Is Composed of Enterprise Goals, and Is Composed of Enterprise Objectives. The Element Type includes Driver, Enterprise Goal, Enterprise Objective, and Actual Strategic Phase.</p><h3>Variant Map</h3><p>The<span> </span><strong>Variant Map</strong><span> </span>depicts the specializations of a Resource Performer, including Capability Configuration, Software, and other elements. Specializations of a Resource Performer are modeled using the Generalization relationship. </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722680 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01102: Typical Resources Deployment Wizard

- page_id: `136722680`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722680/Typical+Resources+Deployment+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-03-06T14:43:13.237+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** is to deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.

To open the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space=''] (DoDAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space=''] (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space=''] (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space=''] (UAF) table.
2. Double-click a cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

none

##### 1. Select Resources

In this step, select the Resourcesrequired by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing Resources or create new ones.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### 2. In Service date specification

In this step, you can specify the In Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be released and used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default, the Today date is selected.

If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase) and the date selection steps are not available.

The In Service date for the Resource usage can not be later than the Out Of Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard only if the start date is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### 3. Out Of Service date specification

In this step, you can specify the Out Of Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be withdrawn. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.

The Out Of Service date for the Resourceusage can not be earlier than the In Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard if the end date is not specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestones with the same chosen dates already exist, they are reused.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> is to deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> do the following</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with StV-5 typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (MODAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NCV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF), or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Typical Deployment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (UAF) table.</li><li>Double-click a cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears on the right side of the cell.<br />The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Organizational Resource </span>responsible for that Capability deployment.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17c9cf5d-37bf-4906-a7e8-f6df9748397f"><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>1. Select Resources</h3><p>In this step, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources </span>required by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources</span></span> or create new ones.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resource_deployment_wizard_I.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>2. In Service date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the In Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be released and used<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Setting</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. By default, the Today date is selected.</p><p>If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase)</span> and the date selection steps are not available. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2aeaf087-6e32-4bfb-8357-ed8801f7246a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The In Service date for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resource</span> usage can not be later than the Out Of Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> wizard only if the start date is specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resource_deployment_wizard_II.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>3. Out Of Service date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the Out Of Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be withdrawn<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22148d28-e480-4577-83a3-a50b5eb00146"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Out Of Service date for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resource </span>usage can not be earlier than the In Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> wizard if the end date is not specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resource_deployment_wizard_III.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestones with the same chosen dates already exist, they are reused.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with StV-5 typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723455 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01103: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Changes of element type and metaclass

- page_id: `136723455`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723455/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+of+element+type+and+metaclass
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.694+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.

| Element | UAF 1.0 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.2 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AssetRole | Element | Property |
| Desirer | Class | Element |
| WholeLifeEnterprise | Class | InstanceSpecification |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.0 [Type] | UAF 1.2 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity::activityPerformableUnderCondition | Condition | ActualCondition |
| EnterpriseGoal::benefit | String | ValueItem |
| Measurement::environmentalContext | Condition | ActualCondition |
| LocationHolder::requiredEnvironment | Environment | ActualEnvironment |
| CapabilityConfiguration::doctrine | Constraint | StandardOperationalActivity |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.</span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.0 [Metaclass]</p></th><th><p><span>UAF 1</span>.2 [Metaclass]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="15028440-6dcb-486e-bef1-35f24fd0b13e">AssetRole</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Element</span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Property</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f7504881-3372-4728-b065-260c535eaabb">Desirer</ac:inline-comment-marker></td><td colspan="1">Class</td><td colspan="1">Element</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">WholeLifeEnterprise</td><td colspan="1">Class</td><td colspan="1">InstanceSpecification</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h4><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="55b6d176-05eb-4bf4-aca8-b9bc3d6ce51a">Changes types of Elements</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p>The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element/Property</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.0 [Type]</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.2 [Type]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1">Activity::<em>activityPerformableUnderCondition</em></td><td colspan="1">Condition </td><td colspan="1">ActualCondition</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">EnterpriseGoal::<em>benefit</em></td><td colspan="1">String</td><td colspan="1">ValueItem</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1b2a846f-fdaa-4135-8a89-f3caa6c97602">Measurement::</ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1b2a846f-fdaa-4135-8a89-f3caa6c97602">environmentalContext</ac:inline-comment-marker></em> </td><td colspan="1">Condition</td><td colspan="1"><span>ActualCondition</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c8fbf0e-7163-4f1f-91b8-5a36f94a407c">LocationHolder::</ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c8fbf0e-7163-4f1f-91b8-5a36f94a407c">requiredEnvironment</ac:inline-comment-marker></em> </td><td colspan="1">Environment</td><td colspan="1">ActualEnvironment</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="93f1dd43-1837-4f38-bd35-7802194480ce">CapabilityConfiguration::</ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="93f1dd43-1837-4f38-bd35-7802194480ce">doctrine</ac:inline-comment-marker></em></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="93f1dd43-1837-4f38-bd35-7802194480ce">Constraint</ac:inline-comment-marker></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="93f1dd43-1837-4f38-bd35-7802194480ce">StandardOperationalActivity</ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723458 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01104: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `136723458`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723458/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.983+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Known Resource exception

946151309

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Known Resource exception

946151320

946151307

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Known Resource exception

946151310

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

##### Changed Property Types

44

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for :

- Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.
- Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created . I f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions, the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

##### The Security Property renamed

In UAF 1.2, the Security Property is renamed to Operational Information Role and the new Resource Information Role is implemented. The Operational Information Role is used by the Operational Assets and the Resource Information Role is used by the Resource Assets.

- Operational Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information.
- Resource Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information.

If Operational Agent and Operational Information (in UAF 1.2 grouped by Operational Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Operational Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was any of the Operational Asset specializations.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If Resource Performer and Resource Information (in UAF 1.2 grouped by Resource Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Resource Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Operational Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Known Resource exception

Known Resource exception

The Known Resource is both: Resource Asset and Operational Asset. After the migration, the Security Property (owned by the Know Resource) is changed depending on its type:

- If the type was Resource Information, the Security Property becomes Resource Information Role.
- If the type was Operational Information, the Security Property becomes Operational Information Role.

##### Enduring Task removed

Enduring Task element was removed, so after the migration, it become Strategic Phase.

##### Capability For Task removed

Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse).

##### Changes for properties of ActualEnterprisePhase

- Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Operational Architecture element.
- Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Resource Architecture element.
- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal
- **Concern**property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between *Actual Enterprise Phase* and Concern

##### Changes for *Concern property*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Concern

##### Changes for property of *Enterprise Goal*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal

##### Changes for *property of* *Enterprise Vision*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision

##### Changes for properties of Desired Effect and Achieved Effect

- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect is removed.
- DesiredEffect:: achievedEffect is removed.
- After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between **desires** target and **achieves** target if AchievedEffect:: **desiredEffect** and DesiredEffect::**achievedEffect** were linked with each other.

##### Consumes renamed

In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports and the direction is reversed.

##### Specializations of Capable Element updated

In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, Exhibits relationship was removed and Phases will be created.

##### Changed Property Types

In earlier versions, the Benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2 benefit property has value Item for its value. After migration, the Value Item element will be created based on benefit value.

##### Changed for Whole Life Enterprise

In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723456 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01105: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `136723456`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723456/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.790+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Operational Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information allowing them to own Operational Information Role. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Resource Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information allowing them to own Resource Information Roles |
| Resource Information Role* | A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements. |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange. The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an &quot;end effect&quot;.</td></tr><tr><td>Capability Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge</td><td>An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Compares To</td><td>An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result.</td></tr><tr><td>Creates</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver</td><td>A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Effect</td><td>A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes.</td></tr><tr><td>Enables</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Mission</td><td>Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Objective</td><td>A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Evoked By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Governed By</td><td>An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs.</td></tr><tr><td>Impacted By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Maps To Goal</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Motivated By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Asset</td><td>An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information allowing them to own Operational Information Role.</td></tr><tr><td>Opportunity</td><td>An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges.</td></tr><tr><td>Owns Value</td><td>An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item.</td></tr><tr><td>Phases</td><td>An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. </td></tr><tr><td>Presented By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Asset</td><td>An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information allowing them to own Resource Information Roles</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Information Role*</td><td>A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service</td><td>A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service Interface</td><td>A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize.</td></tr><tr><td>Security Risk</td><td>The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].</td></tr><tr><td>Sequence</td><td>A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Architecture</td><td>An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Contract</td><td>A constraint governing the use of one or more Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Control Flow</td><td>An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange</td><td>Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Item</td><td>An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Kind</td><td><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange. The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:</p><ul><li>Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Service Object Flow</td><td>An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal</td><td>A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal Property</td><td>A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Constraint</td><td>A Rule governing a Capability.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Exchange</td><td>Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Information</td><td>Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item</td><td>An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Stream</td><td>An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">* For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ac:link>.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723457 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01106: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `136723457`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723457/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.887+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.0 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Capability Property | Capability Role |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Security Property* | Operational Information Role |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'].

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element | Property UAF 1.0 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| OperationalExchange | trustlevel | trustLevel |
| ResponsibleFor | ResponsibleRoleKind | responsibleRoleKind |
| Architecture | desribedBy | describedBy |
| ArchitecturalDescription | viewPoint | viewpoint |

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- Condition::conditionKind
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'].

## PAGE 01107: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Changes of element metaclass and type

- page_id: `136723441`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723441/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+of+element+metaclass+and+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.101+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element | UAF 1.1 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.2 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| WholeLifeEnterprise | Class | InstanceSpecification |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.1 [Type] | UAF 1.2 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| EnterpriseGoal::benefit | String | ValueItem |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723444 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01108: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `136723444`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723444/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.291+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

950009550

950009571

950009548

**On this page**

950009560

##### Enduring Task removed

The Enduring Task element was removed, so it became Strategic Phase after the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability For Task removed

Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration, Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of ActualEnterprisePhase

- Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Operational Architecture element.
- Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Resource Architecture element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal
- **Concern**property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between *Actual Enterprise Phase* and Concern

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of Desired Effect and Achieved Effect

- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect is removed.
- DesiredEffect:: achievedEffect is removed.
- After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between **desires** target and **achieves** target if AchievedEffect:: **desiredEffect** and DesiredEffect::**achievedEffect** were linked with each other.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Consumes renamed

In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports, and the direction is reversed.

##### Risk Owner removed

The Risk Owner property is removed, so after the migration, it will be added to the ToDo property in the Specification window of the Risk element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specializations of Capable Element updated

In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes Property Types

In earlier versions, the benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2, the benefit property has a value Item for its value.After migration, a value item element will be created based on benefit value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for Whole Life Enterprise

In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="66cc6c20-5e6a-4e90-b897-28d6e777c5e5">Changes for properties of  Desired Effect and Achieved Effect</ac:inline-comment-marker> </h3><ul><li>AchievedEffect:: <strong>desiredEffect</strong> is removed.</li><li>DesiredEffect::<strong>achievedEffect</strong> is removed.</li><li><p>After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between <strong>desires</strong> target and <strong>achieves</strong> target if AchievedEffect:: <strong>desiredEffect</strong> and DesiredEffect::<strong>achievedEffect</strong> were linked with each other. </p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="achieves_desires.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Consumes renamed</h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports, and the direction is reversed. </p><h3>Risk Owner removed</h3><p>The Risk Owner property is removed, so after the migration, it will be added to the ToDo property in the Specification window of the Risk element.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="riskOwner_removed.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="06d1ad52-188a-4a7c-911c-e7e0a9542b49">Specializations of Capable Element updated</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exhibits_to_phases.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="230554ab-0e82-49cc-86d0-15b6d571d8e6">Changes Property Types</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions, the benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2, the benefit property <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">has a value Item for its value. </span>After migration, a value item element will be created based on benefit value. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="benefit_to_valueItem.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c23d762c-2384-4095-9a5f-a424f761adb7">Changes for Whole Life Enterprise</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="whole_life_enterprise_to_strategicPhase.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723442 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01109: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `136723442`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723442/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.159+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723443 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01110: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `136723443`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723443/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:12.216+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.1 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Property UAF 1.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |

****

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
