<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721795 space=UAF12P2024x version=6 -->
## PAGE 01111: UAF 1.2

- page_id: `136721795`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721795/UAF+1.2
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:21:21.498+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

769321950

769321952

769321951

The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:

- model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;
- model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;
- support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and
- improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML based and tools that are based on other standards.

According to modeling needs, there are two UAF templates for different purposes:

- UAF Project is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.
- EA with BPMN Project* - u sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.
- **UAF Project Template with Process Guide**- using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.
- **EA Project Template with Process Guide**- using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.
- **Blank UAF Project**- creates a blank UAF project.

Please make sure, that the UAF Plugin is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='NMDOC'].

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.2 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification.

The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:

| Viewpoint | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Architecture Management | Am | Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose. |
| Strategic | St | Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution. |
| Operational | Op | Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner. |
| Services | Sv | The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service withinMODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas. |
| Personnel | Ps | Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time. |
| Resources | Rs | Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML or UML. |
| Security | Sc | Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves). |
| Projects | Pj | Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects. |
| Standards | Sd | MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements. |
| Actual Resources | Ar | The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations. |

The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:

| Aspect | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Motivation | Mv | Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls. |
| Taxonomy | Tx | Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. |
| Structure | Sr | Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts. |
| Connectivity | Cn | Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements. |
| Processes | Pr | Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them. |
| States | St | Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions. |
| Sequences | Sq | Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario. |
| Information | If | Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues. |
| Parameters | Pm | Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems. |
| Constraints | Ct | Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure. |
| Roadmap | Rm | Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time. |
| Traceability | Tr | Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors. |

769321949

**UAF 1.2 viewpoints**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8682665-1f87-49ab-a24f-5d44fbf3bcae"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">769321950</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c63309a-12b7-4ce1-acfa-b23b319c91f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">769321952</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1945d0e0-6ac7-4dd2-a61b-c2ca74b3618d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">769321951</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:</p><ul><li>model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;</li><li>model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;</li><li>support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and</li><li>improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML based and tools that are based on other standards.</li></ul><p>According to modeling needs, there are two UAF templates for different purposes:</p><ul><li><strong>UAF Project</strong><span> </span>is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.</li><li><strong>EA with BPMN Project*<span> </span></strong>- u<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>UAF Project Template with Process Guide</strong><span> </span>- using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>EA Project Template with Process Guide</strong><span> </span>- using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Blank UAF Project</strong><span> </span>- creates a blank UAF project.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36302a13-0993-4e01-9260-10ef8aff2505"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please make sure, that the UAF Plugin is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="Installing plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><hr /><p>*Starting with the 2024x version, the <strong style="text-align: left;">UAF Enterprise Architecture Project</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>was renamed to<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">EA with BPMN Project.</strong></p><p><strong style="text-align: left;">      </strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">UAF 1.2 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification. </h6><p>The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 93.8014%;"><colgroup> <col style="width: 7.89936%;" /> <col style="width: 4.73961%;" /> <col style="width: 87.361%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p align="center"><strong>Viewpoint</strong></p></th><th><p align="center"><strong>Acronym</strong></p></th><th><p align="center"><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr><th><p>Architecture Management</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Am</p></td><td><p>Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Strategic</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>St</p></td><td><p>Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Operational</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Op</p></td><td><p>Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Services</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Sv</p></td><td><p>The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service within</p><p>MODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.</p><p>DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Personnel</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Ps</p></td><td><p>Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Resources</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Rs</p></td><td><p>Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML or UML.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Security</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Sc</p></td><td><p>Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves).</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Projects</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Pj</p></td><td><p>Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Standards</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Sd</p></td><td><p>MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.</p><p>DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements.</p></td></tr><tr><th><p>Actual Resources</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Ar</p></td><td><p>The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&amp;V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 94.0208%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 7.82253%;" /><col style="width: 5.02043%;" /><col style="width: 87.157%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;">Aspect</th><th style="text-align: center;">Acronym</th><th style="text-align: center;">Description</th></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Motivation</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Mv</td><td colspan="1">Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls.</td></tr><tr><th>Taxonomy</th><td style="text-align: center;">Tx</td><td><p>Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the<strong> </strong>source of the element.</p></td></tr><tr><th>Structure</th><td style="text-align: center;">Sr</td><td><p>Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts.</p></td></tr><tr><th>Connectivity</th><td style="text-align: center;">Cn</td><td><p>Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements.</p></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Processes</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Pr</td><td colspan="1"><p>Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them.</p></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">States</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">St</td><td colspan="1"><p>Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions.</p></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Sequences</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Sq</td><td colspan="1"><p>Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario.</p></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Information</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">If</td><td colspan="1"><p>Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues.</p></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Parameters</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Pm</td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems.</span></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Constraints</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Ct</td><td colspan="1"><p>Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure.</p></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Roadmap</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Rm</td><td colspan="1"><p>Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time.</p></td></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Traceability</th><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Tr</td><td colspan="1"><p>Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1915f16d-c405-4c3a-ab72-0e268908ad76"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">769321949</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>UAF 1.2 viewpoints</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722853 space=UAF12P2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 01115: UAF Behavior to structure synchronization

- page_id: `136722853`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722853/UAF+Behavior+to+structure+synchronization
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-03-19T12:58:03.535+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity diagram with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies, and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solver actions to ensure the coherence of the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity diagrams with Activities.

600400

The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Operational / Resource / Service Role displayed in the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity Diagram to check for compatible

- Operational / Resource / Service Ports
- Operational / Resource / Service Connectors
- Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces
- Operational / Resource / Service Exchanges

To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., ports, interfaces) and behavioral elements (e.g., flows) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Connectivity Diagram and displayed in the**Validation Results** panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing behavior to the structure.

##### Checking behavior to structure synchronization

To initiate the behavior to structure synchronization check, ensure the Internal Connectivity Diagrams have

displayed,

).

To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model

1. In the toolbar of the <viewpoint name> * Internal Connectivity Diagram, click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools.
2. In the open drop-down menu, select Behavior to Structure Synchronization. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.

*** <Viewpoint name>** - Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).

**Continue reading**

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the &lt;viewpoint name&gt;<strong>*</strong> Internal Connectivity diagram with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies, and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solver actions to ensure the coherence of the &lt;viewpoint name&gt;<strong>*</strong> Internal Connectivity diagrams with Activities.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="358938f0-c5d2-4ee2-8419-7113fa7ab391"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="http://youtube.com/watch?v=Oxq3gFqS1Qc" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>   </p><p>The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Operational / Resource / Service Role displayed in the &lt;viewpoint name&gt;<strong>*</strong> Internal Connectivity Diagram to check for compatible</p><ul><li>Operational / Resource / Service Ports</li><li>Operational / Resource / Service Connectors</li><li>Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces</li><li>Operational / Resource / Service Exchanges</li></ul><p>To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., ports, interfaces) and behavioral elements (e.g., flows) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Connectivity Diagram and displayed in the<strong> Validation Results</strong> panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1419"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF BtoSSync Reprezentation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing behavior to the structure.</h6><h3>Checking behavior to structure synchronization </h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c9205c4-d737-42c3-afbf-a049507df07d"><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To initiate the behavior to structure synchronization check, ensure the Internal Connectivity Diagrams have </span>Asset Roles<em style="text-align: left;"> </em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">displayed,</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> and the behavior model is defined (in the Process Flow diagrams, the communication between<span> </span></span>Asset Roles is modeled<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">).</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model</p><hr /><ol><li>In the toolbar of the &lt;viewpoint name&gt;<strong>*</strong> Internal Connectivity Diagram, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools_button.png" /></ac:image>Tools.</li><li>In the open drop-down menu, select <strong>Behavior to Structure Synchronization.</strong><br /><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_sync_button.png" /></ac:image><br /></strong></li></ol><p>The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated. </p><p>  </p><hr /><p><strong>* &lt;Viewpoint name&gt;</strong> - Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Continue reading</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Abstract super type for all of the UAF elements. It provides a way for all of the UAF elements to have a common set of properties.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

Element

## PAGE 01117: UAF projects migration manual

- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

The following chapters describe how migration works.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723422 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01118: UAF specific elements

- page_id: `136723422`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723422/UAF+specific+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:11.067+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Metaclass | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Association | Operational Association | A relationship summarizing Operational Exchanges between connected Operational Performers. |
| Resource Association | A relationship summarizing Resource Exchanges between connected Resources. |  |
| Call Behavior Action | Operational Action | A Call Behavior Action for which the default type will not be created. However if you need to create or set a type for this element, you can select or create only an Operational Activity. |
| Resource Action | A Call Behavior Action for which the default type will not be created. However if you need to create or set a type for this element, you can select or create only a Function. |  |
| Service Action | A Call Behavior Action for which the default type will not be created. However if you need to create or set a type for this element, you can select or create only a Service Function. |  |
| Class | Time Period | An element which contains Date elements. |
| Dependency | Arbitrary Relationship Dashed Left | A relationship which you can used in a Free Form diagram to show dependencies. |
| Arbitrary Relationship Dashed Right |  |  |
| Arbitrary Relationship Directed Left |  |  |
| Arbitrary Relationship Directed Right |  |  |
| Arbitrary Relationship Solid |  |  |
| Property | Agreement Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Agreement. |
| Capability Configuration Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Capability Configuration. |  |
| Condition Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Condition. |  |
| Environment Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Environment. |  |
| Location Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Location. |  |
| Natural Resource Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Natural Resource. |  |
| Operational Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Operational Performer. |  |
| Organization Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Organization. |  |
| Person Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Person. |  |
| Post Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Post. |  |
| Resource Artifact Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Resource Artifact. |  |
| Software Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Software. |  |
| Technology Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Technology. |  |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723518 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01120: UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0

- page_id: `136723518`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723518/UPDM+1.x+Migration+to+UPDM+2.0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:19.409+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### How to Migrate

You will be suggested migrating project each time you open the UPDM 1.x version project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Before starting the mapping procedure, please consider the following:

- The project does not use any UPDM 1.x version modules. If it does, please convert modules one by one before migrating the project.
- You have made a copy of your project.
- You have read this manual.

If you agree migrating the project, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3>How to Migrate</h3><p>You will be suggested migrating project each time you open the UPDM 1.x version project.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UPDM Migration Manual.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Before starting the mapping procedure, please consider the following:</p><ul><li>The project does not use any UPDM 1.x version modules. If it does, please convert modules one by one before migrating the project.</li><li>You have made a copy of your project.</li><li>You have read this manual.</li></ul><p>If you agree migrating the project, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723516 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01121: UPDM 2.0 Migration to UPDM 2.1

- page_id: `136723516`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723516/UPDM+2.0+Migration+to+UPDM+2.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:19.310+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### How to Migrate

The migration from UPDM 2.0 to UPDM 2.1 will be performed automatically. During the migration, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3>How to Migrate</h3><p>The migration from UPDM 2.0 to UPDM 2.1 will be performed automatically. During the migration, autocheck of the used modules will be performed. You will be warned if there are modules to be converted. You will be able to cancel the migration procedure.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e9ea9f7e-96a6-439d-aa3e-06e9476c2e5b" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723496 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01122: UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1

- page_id: `136723496`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723496/UPDM+2.1+migration+to+UAF+1.1
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:16:26.355+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1265258636

1265258639

1265258637

##### UAF definition

The Unified Profile for DoDAF and MODAF (UAF) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF).

##### The purpose of UAF migration

The purpose of UAF migration to the latest UAF specification version is to convert earlier UAF versions metamodel-based projects into the latest UAF version metamodel-based projects without losing data.

Migration back to earlier UAF versions is not possible, so we highly recommend you to back-up your current UAFprojects before proceeding with migration to the latest UAFversion.

##### The purpose of the migration guide

The purpose of this guide is to help you to understand the differences between UAFspecifications and guide you along the data mappingprocess.

This manual describes the data mapping processwhile migrating from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.1.

1265258635

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723481 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01126: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `136723481`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723481/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:15.595+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Elements mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Achieved Effect | A dependency relationship that exists between an Actual State (e.g., observed/measured during testing) of an element that attempts to achieve a Desired Effect and an Achiever. |
| Achiever | An Actual Resource, Actual Project or Actual Enterprise Phase that can deliver a Desired Effect. |
| Actual Condition | The Actual State of an environment or location describing its situation. |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Enduring Task | An actual undertaking recognized by an enterprise as being essential to achieving its goals - i.e. a strategic specification of what the enterprise does. |
| Actual Enterprise Phase | An Actual State that describes the phase of an Enterprise endeavour. |
| Actual Environment | The Actual State that describes the circumstances of an Environment. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Actual Project Role | An Actual Project that is applied to a Project Role. |
| Actual Resource | Role in an Organization, where the role carries the authority to undertake a function - though the Actual Organizational Resource given the role has the responsibility. |
| Actual Resource Role | An instance of a System Resource. |
| Actual Responsibility | The duty required of a Person or Organization. |
| Actual Responsible Resource | An abstract grouping of responsible Organizational Resources. |
| Actual Service | An instance of a Service Specification. |
| Actual State | Abstract element that applies temporal extent to a set of elements realized as Instance Specifications. |
| Architecture | An abstract element that represents a generic architecture. Subtypes are Logical Architecture and Physical Architecture. |
| Asset | Asset as applied to Security views, an abstract element that indicates the types of elements that can be considered as a subject for security analysis. |
| Asset Role | Asset Role as applied to Security views, an abstract element that indicates the type of elements that can be considered as a subject for security analysis in the particular context. |
| Capability For Task | An abstraction relationship that asserts that a Capability is required in order for an Enterprise to conduct a phase of an Enduring Task. |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Competence To Conduct | An abstraction relationship used to associate a Function with a specific set of Competencies needed to conduct the Function |
| Concern | Interest in an Enterprise Phase (Enterprise Phase is synonym for System in ISO 42010) relevant to one or more of its stakeholders. |
| Consumes | A Consumes relationship is an abstraction relationship that asserts that a service in someway contributes or assists in the execution of an Operational Activity. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Data Role | A usage of Data Element that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Data Elements. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enhances | A dependency relationship relating the Tailored Security Control to a Security Control. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Fielded Capability | An actual, fully-realized capability. A Fielded Capability is typed by a Capability Configuration. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Information Role | A usage of Information Element that exists in the context of an Operational Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Information Elements. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Measurable Element | Abstract grouping for elements that can be measured by applying Measurement Sets to them. |
| Mitigation Role | An abstract element that indicates the types of elements that can be considered as a subject for mitigating against a risk. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Operational Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Information Element allowing them to own Information Roles. |
| Operational Interface | A declaration that specifies a contract between the Nodes it is related to and any other Nodes it can interact with. |
| Operational Mitigation | A set of security measures intended to address against specific cyber risks. Comprises a subset of Security Controls that are required to protect the asset at node (Operational Role). |
| Operational Signal | An Operational Signal is a specification of a kind of communication between operational performers in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Organization In Enterprise | An abstraction relationship relating an Actual Organization to an Actual Enterprise Phase to denote that the Actual Organization plays a role or is a stakeholder in an Actual Enterprise Phase. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Performs In Context | An abstraction relationship that relates an Operational Action to a Operational Role, or a Function Action to a Resource Role. It indicates that the action can be carried out by the role when used in a specific context or configuration. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Project Activity | An activity carried out during a project. |
| Project Activity Action | The Project Activity Action is defined as a call behavior action that invokes the activity that needs to be preformed. |
| Project Role | Usage of a Project in the context of another Project. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Protects | A dependency that asserts that a Security Control is required to protect an Asset. |
| Protects In Context | A dependency relationship that relates a Security Control Action to a Operational Role, or a Resource Role. It indicates that Security Control is required to protect an Asset in a specific context or configuration. |
| Protocol Stack | A sub-type of Protocol that contains the Protocol Layers, defining a complete stack. |
| Required Service Level | A sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level required of the provider. |
| Resource Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Data Element allowing them to own Data Roles |
| Resource Interface | A declaration that specifies a contract between the System Resources it is related to and any other System Resources it can interact with. It is also intended to be an implementation of a specification of an Interface in the Business and/or Service layer. |
| Resource Mitigation | A set of security measures intended to address specific cyber risks. Comprises a subset of Tailored Security Controls that are used to protect the asset at resource (Resource Role). |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Resource Signal | A Resource Signal is a specification of a kind of communication between resources (ResourcePerformers) in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Risk | A statement of the impact of an event on Assets. It represents a constraint on an Asset in terms of adverse effects, with an associated measure of the likelihood of the event’s occurrence. Software related security risks are those risks that arise from the loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability of information or information systems. |
| Security Constraint | A type of rule that captures a formal statement to define access control policy language. |
| Security Control | A type of Operational Activity that specifies a safeguard or countermeasure prescribed for Operational Performer. It is intended to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of its information. |
| Security Control Action | A call of a Security Control in the context of another Security Control. It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset at node (Operation Role). |
| Security Control Family | An element that organizes security controls into a family. |
| Security Endave | An element that is a collection of information systems connected by one or more internal networks under the control of a single authority and security policy. The systems may be structured by physical proximity or by function, independent of location. |
| Security Process | The security-related procedure that satisfies the security control requirement. |
| Security Process Action | A call of a Security Control in the context of another Security Control. It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset at node (Node Role). |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Connector | A channel for exchange between two Service Specifications. Where one acts as the consumer of the other. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kindsEnumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Service Specification | The specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others. |
| Service Specification Role | Usage of a Service Specification in the context of another Service Specification. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Subject Of Risk | An abstract grouping of elements that can be the subject of a Risk. |
| Subject Of Security Constraint | An abstract grouping of elements that can be the subject of a Security Constraint. |
| Tailored Security Control | A type of Function that implements a Security Control, it specifies a safeguard or countermeasure prescribed for a System Resource. It is intended to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of its information. |
| Technology | A sub type of Resource Artifact that indicates a technology domain, i.e. nuclear, mechanical, electronic, mobile telephony etc. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |
| Version Succession | A dependency relationship between two Version Of Configurations that denotes that one Version Of Configuration follows from another. |
| Versioned Element | An abstract grouping of System Resource and Service Specification that allows Version Of Configuration to be related to Actual Project Milestones. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Service Object Flow</td><td>An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal</td><td>A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal Property</td><td>A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Specification</td><td>The specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Specification Role</td><td>Usage of a Service Specification in the context of another Service Specification. Creates a whole-part relationship.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Constraint</td><td>A Rule governing a Capability.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Exchange</td><td>Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Information</td><td>Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action.</td></tr><tr><td>Subject Of Risk</td><td>An abstract grouping of elements that can be the subject of a Risk.</td></tr><tr><td>Subject Of Security Constraint</td><td>An abstract grouping of elements that can be the subject of a Security Constraint.</td></tr><tr><td>Tailored Security Control</td><td>A type of Function that implements a Security Control, it specifies a safeguard or countermeasure prescribed for a System Resource. It is intended to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of its information.</td></tr><tr><td>Technology</td><td>A sub type of Resource Artifact that indicates a technology domain, i.e. nuclear, mechanical, electronic, mobile telephony etc.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item</td><td>An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Stream</td><td>An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</td></tr><tr><td>Version Succession</td><td>A dependency relationship between two Version Of Configurations that denotes that one Version Of Configuration follows from another.</td></tr><tr><td>Versioned Element</td><td>An abstract grouping of System Resource and Service Specification that allows Version Of Configuration to be related to Actual Project Milestones.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723480 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01127: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Removed elements and stereotypes

- page_id: `136723480`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723480/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Removed+elements+and+stereotypes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:15.259+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Elements mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All removed elements are no longer used in UAF 1.2 and do not map to the other elements.

If the element, element property, or relationship does not exists in UAF 1.2, the additional information is added into the migrated element's To Do property. For more information about how to use To Do property, see in the page [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using To Do property' space='UAF12P2024x'].

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.2 |
| --- | --- |
| Activity Part Of Project | Dependency |
| Activity Subject |  |
| Asynchronous Message | Signal |
| Association Of Information | Association |
| Competence Provider |  |
| Competence Requirer |  |
| Condition Kind |  |
| Condition Property | Property |
| Condition Type |  |
| Contract [Information Flow] | Information Flow |
| Design Rule |  |
| Desired State |  |
| Details | Dependency |
| Enduring Task |  |
| Entity Attribute | Property |
| Entity Item | Operational Information |
| Entity Relationship | Association |
| External Individual |  |
| External Type |  |
| External Tuple |  |
| External Tuple Type |  |
| Geo Political Extent | Instance Specification |
| Known Resource | Operational Role |
| Mission | Operational Activity |
| Ontology Reference |  |
| Operational Event Trace | Interaction |
| Operational State | State |
| Overlap |  |
| Participant | Class |
| Process |  |
| Project | Actual Project |
| Resource Event Trace | Interaction |
| Resource State | State |
| Semantic | Data Type |
| Semantic Attribute | Property |
| Service Description | Architectural Description |
| Service Feature |  |
| Service Function Edge | Service Object FlowService Control Flow |
| Service Interaction | Interaction |
| Service Level Value | Actual Measurement |
| Service Message Handler | Reception |
| Standard Configuration | Comment without stereotype |
| Stereotype Extension | Comment |
| Subject Of Operational State Machine |  |
| Transactional | Data Type |
| Transactional Attribute | Property |
| View |  |
| Viewpoint |  |
| Wrapper | Class |
| Wrapper Attribute | Property |

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
