<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723479 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01128: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and replaced elements

- page_id: `136723479`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723479/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+replaced+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:15.060+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Elements mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The replaced elements mean that their names and/or metaclasses are changed. Pay attention, that some elements from UPDM 2.1 are replaced to one element in UAF 1.2.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.2 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity Part Of Capability [Dependency] | Maps To Capability [Abstraction] | Maps To Capability relationship denotes that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability. |
| Activity Performed By Performer [Dependency]Is Capable Of Performing [Dependency] | Is Capable To Perform [Abstraction] | Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform. |
| Actual Organization Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship is an abstract element that details the Actual Organizational Resources that are able to carry out an Actual Responsibility. |
| Actual Property | Actual Measurement | Actual Measurement is an actual value that is applied to a Measurement. |
| Capability Of Performer [Dependency] | Exhibits [Abstraction] | Exhibits relationship that exists between a capable element and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions. |
| Capability Property | Capability Role | A high level specification of the enterprise's ability to execute a specified course of action. |
| ClimateLight Condition | Environment | A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind. |
| Desired Effect | Desires | A dependency relationship relating the Desirer (a Capability or OrganizationalResource) to an ActualState. |
| Deployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneOut Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneActual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone is an event with a start date in an Actual Project from which progress is measured. Actual Project Milestone can have such kinds:In Service.Deployed.No Longer Used.Out Of Service.Other. |
| EnergyMateriel | Natural Resource | Natural Resource is any type of physical resource that occurs in nature such as oil, water, gas, or coal. |
| Entity Item | Operational Information | An item of information that flows between OperationalPerformers and is produced and consumed by the OperationalActivities that the OperationalPerformers are capable to perform (see IsCapableToPerform). |
| Exchange Element | Resource InformationOperational Information | If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Resource Interaction, it is migrated to Data Element. The Data Element is a formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between systems.If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Operational Exchange or wasn't conveyed, it is migrated to Information Element. The Information Element is an item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform). |
| Expose | Dependency | A Dependency relationship will be used instead of Expose relationship. |
| Functional Standard | Standard | A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Function Edge [Activity Edge] | Function Control Flow [Control Flow]Function Object Flow [Object Flow] | Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow.Function Object Flow connects Function Actions using Pins.Function Control Flow connects Function Actions directly. |
| Project | Actual Project | Actual Project defines a time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of Actual Resources that meet specific Capability needs. |
| Individual Person Role | Actual Post | An actual, specific post, is an instance of a Post "type" - e.g., "President of the United States of America" where the Post would be president. |
| Location | Actual Location | Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc. |
| Location Type | Location | Location element denote a specification of the generic area in which a location holder is required to be located. |
| Logical Architecture | Operational Architecture | Operational Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the architecture, described from the business operational perspective. |
| Measure | Actual Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set denote a set of Actual Measurements. |
| Measure TypeSecurity Attributes Group | Measurement Set | Measurement Set denote a set of Measurements. |
| Milestone Sequence | Milestone Dependency | Milestone Dependency relationship is relationship between two Actual Project Milestones that denotes one Actual Project Milestone follows from another. |
| Mission | Operational Activity | An Activity that captures a logical process, specified independently of how the process is carried out. |
| Needline | Operational Connector | Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges. |
| Node Operation | Operational Method | Operational Method is a behavioral feature of a Operational Performer whose behavior is specified in an Operational Activity. |
| Node Parent | Operational Agent | An abstract element grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer. |
| Node Port | Operational Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created. |
| Node Role | Operational Role | Usage of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Operational Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Operational Control Flow [Control Flow]Operational Object Flow [Object Flow] | Operational Activity Edge is an abstract grouping for Operational Control Flow and Operational Object Flow.Operational Object Flow connects Operational Activity Actions using Pins.Operational Control Flow connects Operational Activity Actions directly. |
| Organizational Project RelationshipProject Ownership | Responsible For | Responsible For relationship is relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For. |
| Organization Type | Organization | Organization element denote a group of organizational resources (Persons, Posts, Organizations and Responsibilities) associated for a particular purpose. |
| NodePerformerSecurity Domain | Operational Performer | Operational Performer denote a logical entity that Is Capable To Perform Operational Activities which produce, consume and process resources. |
| Person Type | Post | Post denote a type of job title or position that a person can fill (e.g. Lawyer, Solution Architect, Machine Operator or Chief Executive Officer). |
| Physical Architecture | Resource Architecture | Resource Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the System Resource perspective. |
| Project Type | Project | Project is an element that describes types of time-limited endeavours that are required to meet one or more Capability needs. |
| Property | Measurement | Measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. |
| RequestService | Operational PortResource Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.If Request port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Request port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port.Resource Port is an interaction point for a System Resource through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a Resource Connector. If Service port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Service port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port. |
| Resource Interaction | Resource Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between ResourcePerformers (i.e. flows of data, people, material, or energy). |
| Resource Interaction Item | Resource Exchange Item | Resource Exchange Item is an abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between System Resources and conveyed by a Resource Exchange. |
| Resource Interface | Resource Connector | Resource Connector is a channel for exchange between two Resource Roles. |
| Resource State Machine | Resource State Description | A state machine describing the behavior of a System Resource, depicting how the System Resource responds to various events and the actions. |
| Resource Operation | Resource Method | A behavioral feature of a ResourcePerformer whose behavior is specified in a Function. |
| Role Type | Responsibility | Responsibility element is the type of duty required of a Person or Organization. |
| Service Access | Resource Service | Capability Configuration represents a composite structure the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a Capability.Service Specification defines the specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others. |
| Service Attribute | Flow Property | Flow Property is a SysML element with already defined direction that is inout. A Flow Property signifies a single flow element that can flow to/from a Block. A flow property’s values are either received from or transmitted to an external Block. Flow properties are defined directly on Blocks or Flow Specifications that are those specifications which type the Flow Ports. Flow properties enable item flows across connectors connecting parts of the corresponding block types, either directly (in case of the property is defined on the Block) or via Flow Ports. |
| Service Channel | Resource ConnectorOperational Connector | If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is System Resource it is be migrated to Resource Connector.If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is Operational Performer it is be migrated to Operational Connector. |
| Service Level Value Set | Provided Service Level | Provided Service Level is a sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level delivered by the provider. |
| Service State Machine | Service State Description | Service State Description is a state machine describing the behavior of a Service Specification, depicting how the Service Specification responds to various events and the actions. |
| Skill | Competence | Competence element is a specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude. |
| Skill Of Person Type | Provides Competence | Provides Competence is a dependency relationship that asserts that an Actual Organizational Resource provides a specific set of Competencies. |
| Standard Configuration | Comment | The Standard Configuration is replaced by the Comment. |
| System | Resource Artifact | Resource Artifact element is a type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.). |
| System Resource | Resource Performer | The Resource Performer covers the Resource Architecture, , Known Resource.Physical Resource |
| Service Operation | Service Method | A behavioral feature of a Service Specification whose behavior is specified in a Service Function. |
| Technical Standard | Standard | Standard is a ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Trustline | Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Vision | Enterprise Vision | An Enterprise Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved. |
| Activity Part Of Project [Dependency]Overlap [Dependency] | Dependency | The Dependency relationship is used instead of stereotyped dependencies such as Activity Part Of Project and Overlap. |
| Association Of Information [Association]Entity Relationship [Association] | Association | The Association relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship. |
| Asynchronous Message [Signal] | Signal | The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message. |
| Condition Property [Property]Entity Attribute [Property] | Property | The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute. |
| Design Rule [Constraint] | Constraint | The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule. |
| Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification] | Instance Specification | The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent . |
| Logical Data Model [Data Model]Physical Data Model [Data Model] | Information Model | The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model. |
| Operational Event Trace [Interaction]Resource Event Trace [Interaction]Service Interaction [Interaction] | Interaction | The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction. |
| Operational State [State]Resource State [State] | State | The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State. |
| Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | ControlFlowObjectFlow | The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge. An Activity Edge is abstract abstract.Object Flow connects using Pins.Control Flow connects directly. |
| Service Description [Package] | Architectural Description | The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description. |
| Service Function Edge [Activity Edge] | Service Control Flow [Control Flow]Service Object Flow [Object Flow] | Service Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Service Control Flow and Service Object Flow.Service Object Flow connects Service Function Actions using Pins.Service Control Flow connects Service Function Actions directly. |
| Service Level Value [Slot] | Slot | The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value. |
| Service Message Handler [Reception] | Reception | The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler. |
| UPDM Element | UAF Element | Abstract super type for all of the UAF elements. It provides a way for all of the UAF elements to have a common set of properties. |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element | Property UPDM 2.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| Property | Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Property | Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Property | Service Specification Role | Service Role |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The replaced elements mean that their names and/or metaclasses are changed. Pay attention, that some elements from UPDM 2.1 are replaced to one element in <span>UAF 1</span>.2.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 94.5694%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 18.3981%;" /><col style="width: 16.8891%;" /><col style="width: 64.7127%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 2.1</th><th><span>UAF 1</span>.2</th><th>Comments</th></tr><tr><td>Activity Part Of Capability [Dependency]</td><td>Maps To Capability [Abstraction]</td><td><p>Maps To Capability relationship denotes that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Activity Performed By Performer [Dependency]</p><p>Is Capable Of Performing [Dependency]</p></td><td colspan="1">Is Capable To Perform [Abstraction]</td><td colspan="1">Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Actual Organization Relationship</td><td colspan="1">Actual Resource Relationship</td><td colspan="1">Actual Resource Relationship is an abstract element that details the Actual Organizational Resources that are able to carry out an Actual Responsibility.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Actual Property</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement is an actual value that is applied to a Measurement.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Capability Of Performer [Dependency]</td><td colspan="1">Exhibits [Abstraction]</td><td colspan="1">Exhibits relationship that exists between a capable element and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Property</span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Role</span></td><td colspan="1">A high level specification of the enterprise's ability to execute a specified course of action.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Climate</p><p>Light Condition</p></td><td colspan="1">Environment</td><td colspan="1">A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Desired Effect</td><td colspan="1">Desires</td><td colspan="1">A dependency relationship relating the Desirer (a Capability or OrganizationalResource) to an ActualState.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="69cd5b13-b74b-44aa-a5b6-3461f70bcf08">Deployed Milestone</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>No Longer Used Milestone</p><p>Out Of Service Milestone</p><p>Increment Milestone</p><p><span>Actual Project Milestone</span></p></td><td colspan="1">Actual Project Milestone</td><td colspan="1"><p>Actual Project Milestone is an event with a start date in an Actual Project from which progress is measured. Actual Project Milestone can have such kinds:</p><ul><li>In Service.</li><li>Deployed.</li><li>No Longer Used.</li><li>Out Of Service.</li><li>Other.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Energy</p><p>Materiel</p></td><td colspan="1">Natural Resource</td><td colspan="1">Natural Resource is any type of physical resource that occurs in nature such as oil, water, gas, or coal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Entity Item</td><td colspan="1">Operational Information</td><td colspan="1">An item of information that flows between OperationalPerformers and is produced and consumed by the OperationalActivities that the OperationalPerformers are capable to perform (see IsCapableToPerform).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Exchange Element</p><p> </p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Resource Information</p><p>Operational Information</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Resource Interaction, it is migrated to Data Element. The Data Element is a formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between systems.</p><p>If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Operational Exchange or wasn't conveyed, it is migrated to Information Element. The Information Element is an item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform).</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Expose</td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">A Dependency relationship will be used instead of Expose relationship.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Functional Standard</td><td colspan="1">Standard</td><td colspan="1">A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Function Edge [Activity Edge]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Function Control Flow [Control Flow]</p><p>Function Object Flow [Object Flow]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow.</p><p>Function Object Flow connects Function Actions using Pins.</p><p>Function Control Flow connects Function Actions directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="54713ff5-5455-4d6b-8b14-1cd1b27e3f79"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d4857158-626d-475e-a994-72c2005a6a32">Project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8d3ddea5-b2ec-441b-a14c-3b51d3cf57c0">Actual Project</ac:inline-comment-marker></td><td colspan="1">Actual Project defines a time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of Actual Resources that meet specific Capability needs.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Individual Person Role</td><td colspan="1">Actual Post</td><td colspan="1">An actual, specific post, is an instance of a Post &quot;type&quot; - e.g., &quot;President of the United States of America&quot; where the Post would be president.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Location</td><td colspan="1">Actual Location</td><td colspan="1">Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Location Type</td><td colspan="1">Location</td><td colspan="1">Location element denote a specification of the generic area in which a location holder is required to be located.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Logical Architecture</td><td colspan="1">Operational Architecture</td><td colspan="1">Operational Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the architecture, described from the business operational perspective.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Measure</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement Set</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement Set denote a set of Actual Measurements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Measure Type</p><p>Security Attributes Group</p></td><td colspan="1">Measurement Set</td><td colspan="1">Measurement Set denote a set of Measurements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Milestone Sequence</td><td colspan="1">Milestone Dependency</td><td colspan="1">Milestone Dependency relationship is relationship between two Actual Project Milestones that denotes one Actual Project Milestone follows from another.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Mission</td><td colspan="1">Operational Activity</td><td colspan="1">An Activity that captures a logical process, specified independently of how the process is carried out.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Needline</td><td colspan="1">Operational Connector</td><td colspan="1">Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Operation</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Method</td><td colspan="1">Operational Method is a behavioral feature of a Operational Performer whose behavior is specified in an Operational Activity.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Parent</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Agent</td><td colspan="1">An abstract element grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Port</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Port</td><td colspan="1">Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Role</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Role</td><td colspan="1">Usage of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Activity Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Control Flow [Control Flow]</p><p>Operational Object Flow [Object Flow]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Activity Edge is an abstract grouping for Operational Control Flow and Operational Object Flow.</p><p>Operational Object Flow connects Operational Activity Actions using Pins.</p><p>Operational Control Flow connects Operational Activity Actions directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Organizational Project Relationship</p><p>Project Ownership</p></td><td colspan="1">Responsible For</td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Responsible For relationship is relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Organization Type</td><td colspan="1">Organization</td><td colspan="1">Organization element denote a group of organizational resources (Persons, Posts, Organizations and Responsibilities) associated for a particular purpose.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Performer</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Security Domain</span></p></td><td colspan="1">Operational Performer</td><td colspan="1">Operational Performer denote a logical entity that Is Capable To Perform Operational Activities which produce, consume and process resources.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Person Type</td><td colspan="1">Post</td><td colspan="1">Post denote a type of job title or position that a person can fill (e.g. Lawyer, Solution Architect, Machine Operator or Chief Executive Officer).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Physical Architecture</td><td colspan="1">Resource Architecture</td><td colspan="1">Resource Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the System Resource perspective.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Project Type</td><td colspan="1">Project</td><td colspan="1">Project is an element that describes types of time-limited endeavours that are required to meet one or more Capability needs.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1">Measurement</td><td colspan="1">Measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Request</p><p>Service</p><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> <br /> </span></p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Port</p><p>Resource Port</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.</p><p>If Request port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.</p><p>If Request port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port.</p><p>Resource Port is an interaction point for a System Resource through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a Resource Connector. <span> </span></p><p>If Service port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.</p><p>If Service port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Operational Port</span>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Resource Interaction</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Resource Exchange</span></td><td colspan="1">Asserts that a flow can exist between ResourcePerformers (i.e. flows of data, people, material, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Interaction Item</td><td colspan="1">Resource Exchange Item</td><td colspan="1">Resource Exchange Item is an abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between System Resources and conveyed by a Resource Exchange.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Interface</td><td colspan="1">Resource Connector</td><td colspan="1">Resource Connector is a channel for exchange between two Resource Roles.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource State Machine</td><td colspan="1">Resource State Description</td><td colspan="1">A state machine describing the behavior of a System Resource, depicting how the System Resource responds to various events and the actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Operation</td><td colspan="1">Resource Method</td><td colspan="1">A behavioral feature of a ResourcePerformer whose behavior is specified in a Function.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Role Type</td><td colspan="1">Responsibility</td><td colspan="1">Responsibility element is the type of duty required of a Person or Organization.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Access</td><td colspan="1"><p>Resource Service</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Capability Configuration represents a composite structure the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a Capability.</p><p>Service Specification defines the specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Attribute</td><td colspan="1">Flow Property</td><td colspan="1"><p>Flow Property is a SysML element with already defined direction that is <em>inout</em>. A Flow Property signifies a single flow element that can flow to/from a Block. A flow property’s values are either received from or transmitted to an external Block. Flow properties are defined directly on Blocks or Flow Specifications that are those specifications which type the Flow Ports. Flow properties enable item flows across connectors connecting parts of the corresponding block types, either directly (in case of the property is defined on the Block) or via Flow Ports.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Channel</td><td colspan="1"><p>Resource Connector</p><p>Operational Connector</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is System Resource it is be migrated to Resource Connector.</p><p>If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is Operational Performer it is be migrated to Operational Connector.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Level Value Set</td><td colspan="1">Provided Service Level</td><td colspan="1">Provided Service Level is a sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level delivered by the provider.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service State Machine</td><td colspan="1">Service State Description</td><td colspan="1">Service State Description is a state machine describing the behavior of a Service Specification, depicting how the Service Specification responds to various events and the actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Skill</td><td colspan="1">Competence</td><td colspan="1">Competence element is a specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Skill Of Person Type</td><td colspan="1">Provides Competence</td><td colspan="1">Provides Competence is a dependency relationship that asserts that an Actual Organizational Resource provides a specific set of Competencies.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Standard Configuration</td><td colspan="1">Comment</td><td colspan="1">The Standard Configuration is replaced by the Comment.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">System</td><td colspan="1">Resource Artifact</td><td colspan="1">Resource Artifact element is a type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">System Resource</td><td colspan="1">Resource Performer</td><td colspan="1">The Resource Performer covers the Resource Architecture, , Known Resource.Physical Resource</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Operation</td><td colspan="1">Service Method</td><td colspan="1">A behavioral feature of a Service Specification whose behavior is specified in a Service Function.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Technical Standard</td><td colspan="1">Standard</td><td colspan="1">Standard is a ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Trustline</td><td colspan="1">Operational Exchange</td><td colspan="1">Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Vision</td><td colspan="1">Enterprise Vision</td><td colspan="1">An Enterprise Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Activity Part Of Project [Dependency]</p><p>Overlap [Dependency]</p></td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The Dependency relationship is used instead of stereotyped dependencies such as Activity Part Of Project and Overlap.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Association Of Information [Association]</p><p>Entity Relationship [Association]</p></td><td colspan="1">Association</td><td colspan="1">The Association relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Asynchronous Message [Signal]</td><td colspan="1">Signal</td><td colspan="1">The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Condition Property [Property]</p><p>Entity Attribute [Property]</p></td><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1">The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Design Rule [Constraint]</td><td colspan="1">Constraint</td><td colspan="1">The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification]</td><td colspan="1">Instance Specification</td><td colspan="1">The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent .</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Logical Data Model [Data Model]</p><p>Physical Data Model [Data Model]</p></td><td colspan="1">Information Model</td><td colspan="1">The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Event Trace [Interaction]</p><p>Resource Event Trace [Interaction]</p><p>Service Interaction [Interaction]</p></td><td colspan="1">Interaction</td><td colspan="1">The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Operational State [State]</p><p>Resource State [State]</p></td><td colspan="1">State</td><td colspan="1">The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1"><p>ControlFlow</p><p>ObjectFlow</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge. An Activity Edge is abstract abstract.</p><p>Object Flow connects using Pins.</p><p>Control Flow connects directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Description [Package]</td><td colspan="1">Architectural Description</td><td colspan="1">The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Function Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Control Flow [Control Flow]</p><p>Service Object Flow [Object Flow]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Service Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Service Control Flow and Service Object Flow.</p><p>Service Object Flow connects Service Function Actions using Pins.</p><p>Service Control Flow connects Service Function Actions directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Level Value [Slot]</td><td colspan="1">Slot</td><td colspan="1">The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Message Handler [Reception]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Reception</p></td><td colspan="1">The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UPDM Element</td><td colspan="1">UAF Element</td><td colspan="1">Abstract super type for all of the UAF elements. It provides a way for all of the UAF elements to have a common set of properties.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p><p>The following table describes properties  which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element</p></th><th><p>Property UPDM 2.1 [name]</p></th><th><p>Property UAF 1.2 [name]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1">Viewpoint</td><td colspan="1"><p>languages</p><p>methods </p></td><td colspan="1"><p>language</p><p>method</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Property</td><td colspan="1"><span>Information Role</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Operational Information Role</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Property</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Data Role</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Resource Information Role</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Property</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Service Specification Role</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Service Role</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723482 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01129: UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. View mapping

- page_id: `136723482`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723482/UPDM+2.1+to+UAF+1.2.+View+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:15.852+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and UAF 1.2.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.2 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Service Channels Summary Table | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |
| Service Channels Summary Matrix | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and <span>UAF 1</span>.2.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 2.1</th><th><span>UAF 1</span>.2</th><th>Comments</th></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Table</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Matrix</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723476 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01130: UPDM projects migration manual

- page_id: `136723476`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723476/UPDM+projects+migration+manual
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:14.565+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of this manual is to help you understanding differences between UPDM specifications and guide you along the data mapping process.

The following chapters describe how the migration works.

##### Basic Concepts and Instructions

The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF).

The purpose of UPDM migration to the latest UPDM specification version is to convert earlier UPDM versions metamodel-based projects into the latest UPDM version metamodel-based projects without losing data.

A migration back to earlier UPDM versions is not possible, so we highly recommend you to back-up your current UPDM projects before proceeding with migration to the latest UPDM version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of this manual is to help you understanding differences between UPDM specifications and guide you along the data mapping process.</p><p>The following chapters describe how the migration works.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c93637d-a5d3-424f-bdae-9a0f68668ae1" /></p><h3>Basic Concepts and Instructions</h3><p>The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF).</p><p>The purpose of UPDM migration to the latest UPDM specification version is to convert earlier UPDM versions metamodel-based projects into the latest UPDM version metamodel-based projects without losing data.</p><p>A migration back to earlier UPDM versions is not possible, so we highly recommend you to back-up your current UPDM projects before proceeding with migration to the latest UPDM version.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722870 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01132: Using BPMN Compliance Mode

- page_id: `136722870`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722870/Using+BPMN+Compliance+Mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:37.772+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

BPMN elements can be integrated into the UAF model. The Processes (BPD) and Sequences (BPD) diagrams in the UAF model are based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).Some diagram elements have both UAF and BPMN stereotypes.

BPMN toolbars are available in the following diagrams:

| Framework | Diagrams |
| --- | --- |
| UAF 1.2 | Operational Process Flow (BPD) |
| DoDAF 2.1 |  |
| MODAF | MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model BPDMODAF. OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPDMODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description BPDMODAF. SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description BPD |
| NAF | NOV-5 Operational Activity Model BPDNOV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPDNSV-4 System Functionality Description BPDNSV-10c System Event-Trace Description BPD |
| NAF 4.0 | L4 Logical Activities BPDL6 Logical Sequence BPDP4 Resource Functions BPDP6 Resource Sequence BPD |

There are two ways to turn the BPMN compliance mode on:

- Creating a new project. In this case, BPMN compliance affects all UAF elements compatible with BPMN.
- Change the Project Options for the existing project. In this case, you can choose if you want the BPMN compliance to affect only new or existing elements.

You will also be suggested to turn the BPMN compliance on while creating the BPD diagram.

To turn on the BPMN compliance mode

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, click the UAF property group.
3. Set the BPMN Compliance Mode property value to true.
4. Click OK .

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### **Mapping UAF and BPMN**

The mapping list of UAF and BPMN stereotypes:

| UAF | BPMN |
| --- | --- |
| Operational Activity Action | Call Activity |
| Function Action | Call Activity |
| Operational Activity | BPMN Process |
| Function | BPMN Process |
| Service Function | BPMN Process |
| Operational Activity Edge [Control Flow] | Sequence Flow |
| Function Edge [Control Flow] | Sequence Flow |
| Operational Activity Edge [Object Flow] | Data Association |
| Function Edge [Object Flow] | Data Association |
| Operational Agent | Resource |
| Resource Performer | Resource |
| Operational Information | BPMN Message |
| Resource Information | BPMN Message |

###### **Example**

Let's see an example of how can BPMN Process elements be used in an Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram.

In the created Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram, the Claim Processing behavior was modeled using the BPMN Process elements. The following figure shows the BPMN Process diagram, where some elements have both UAF and BPMN stereotypes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>BPMN elements can be integrated into the UAF model. The Processes (BPD) and Sequences (BPD) <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">diagrams in the UAF model are based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).<span> Some diagram elements have both UAF and BPMN stereotypes.</span></span></p><p>BPMN toolbars are available in the following diagrams:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Framework</th><th>Diagrams</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>UAF 1.2</p></div></td><td><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Processes" /><ac:link-body><span class="inline-comment-marker">Operational Process Flow (BPD)</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Sequences" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Sequences (BPD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Services Processes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Process Flow (BPD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Personnel Processes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Process Flow (BPD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Personnel Sequences" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Sequences (BPD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resources Processes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Process Flow (BPD) ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resources Sequences" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Sequences (BPD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>DoDAF 2.1</p></div></td><td><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model BPD" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPD" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description BPD" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description BPD" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Functionality Description BPD" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description BPD" /></ac:link></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>MODAF</p></div></td><td><ul><li>MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model BPD</li><li>MODAF. OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPD</li><li>MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description BPD</li><li>MODAF. SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description BPD</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>NAF</p></div></td><td><ul><li>NOV-5 Operational Activity Model BPD</li><li>NOV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description BPD</li><li>NSV-4 System Functionality Description BPD</li><li>NSV-10c System Event-Trace Description BPD</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p> NAF 4.0</p></div></td><td><ul><li>L4 Logical Activities BPD</li><li>L6 Logical Sequence BPD</li><li>P4 Resource Functions BPD</li><li>P6 Resource Sequence BPD</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>There are two ways to turn the BPMN compliance mode on:</p><ul><li>Creating a new project. In this case, BPMN compliance affects <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d7580b35-30b2-4d8b-b03b-dd07e9a519b9">all UAF elements</ac:inline-comment-marker> compatible with BPMN.</li><li>Change the Project Options for the existing project. In this case, you can choose if you want the BPMN compliance to affect only new or existing elements.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c56a2636-9c30-4889-b81a-7e5ccb131b40"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You will also be suggested to turn the BPMN compliance on while creating the BPD diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="43719ce5-0d71-4f64-8b84-d97180955565">To turn on the BPMN</ac:inline-comment-marker> compliance mode </p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, click the <strong>UAF </strong>property group.</li><li>Set the <strong>BPMN Compliance Mode</strong> property value to true.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2023-3-28_14-46-9.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using BPMN Compliance Mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Mapping UAF and BPMN</strong></h4><p style="text-align: left;">The mapping list of UAF and BPMN stereotypes:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UAF</th><th>BPMN</th></tr><tr><td>Operational Activity Action</td><td>Call Activity</td></tr><tr><td>Function Action</td><td>Call Activity</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Activity</td><td>BPMN Process</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Function </td><td colspan="1">BPMN Process</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Function</td><td colspan="1">BPMN Process</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Activity Edge [Control Flow]</td><td colspan="1">Sequence Flow</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Function Edge [Control Flow]</td><td colspan="1">Sequence Flow</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Activity Edge [Object Flow]</td><td colspan="1">Data Association</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Function Edge [Object Flow]</td><td colspan="1">Data Association</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Agent</td><td colspan="1">Resource</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Performer</td><td colspan="1">Resource</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Information</td><td colspan="1">BPMN Message</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Information</td><td colspan="1">BPMN Message</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>Example</strong></h4><p>Let's see an example of how can BPMN Process elements be used in an Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram.</p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the created Operational Process Flow (BPD) diagram, the Claim Processing behavior was modeled using <span>the BPMN Process elements</span>. The following figure shows</span> the BPMN Process diagram, where some elements have both UAF and BPMN stereotypes. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2023-3-28_15-13-44.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using BPMN Compliance Mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722891 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01133: Using drag and drop

- page_id: `136722891`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722891/Using+drag+and+drop
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:38.275+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['drag', 'drop', 'select-drag-drop-rule', 'drag-and-drop']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

162845448

162845468

162845458

Create relationships or assign property values by dragging and dropping one element onto another right in the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

There are a number of predefined rules to create relationships or assign property values automatically. You may also set up your own drag-and-drop specifications between any elements.

To select a rule

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag a selected element onto another, hold it for a few seconds and then release it. A shortcut menu is displayed.
  - Right-click an element and drag it onto another (This action is valid only for Windows operating system users). A shortcut menu is displayed.
2. From the shortcut menu, select one of the rules you want to apply.

The following table lists the predefined drag-and-drop rules. These rules define the possible results when dragging the source element on the target element.

| Source Element | Target Element | Created Relationship | Assigned Property |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Activity | Capability | Maps To Capability |  |
| Actual Condition | Activity |  | Activity Performable Under Condition |
| Actual Condition | Actual Effect |  | Enabling Condition |
| Actual Condition | Actual Resource |  | Actual Condition |
| Actual Condition | Measurement |  | Environmental Context |
| Actual Enduring Task | Actual Enterprise Phase |  | Statement Task |
| Actual Environment | Activity |  | Activity Performable Under Condition |
| Actual Environment | Actual Resource |  | Actual Condition |

| Actual Environment | Measurement |  | Environmental Context |
| Actual Location | Activity |  | Activity Performable Under Condition |

| Actual Organization | Actual Risk |  | Actual Risk Owner |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722894 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01134: Using libraries

- page_id: `136722894`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722894/Using+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:38.464+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['libraries', 'universal-joint-task-list', 'ujtl', 'dod-it-standards-registry', 'disr', 'joint-conditions-library', 'add-standard-policy-from-disr-library']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

715818604

715818606

715818603

**On this page**

715818605

UAF plugin supports three element libraries:

1. The Universal Joint Task List (UJTL).
2. DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry (DISR).
3. Joint Conditions.

The **Universal Joint Task List (UJTL)**, when augmented with the Service task lists, is a comprehensive integrated menu of functional tasks, conditions, measures, and criteria. It supports all levels of the Department of Defense in executing the National Military Strategy. In UAF UJTL library consists of a list of Operational Activities representing universal joint tasks.

The **DoD IT Standards Registry (DISR)** is an online repository of IT standards formerly captured in the Joint Technical Architecture (JTA), Version 6.0. DISR replaces JTA. In UAF, DISR is the list of standard elements that any of UAF element may conform to. DISR Library usage is the same as UJTL library. Library item indexes are included when searching for elements within the element Selection dialog.

The **Joint Conditions Library** supports physical, military, and civil locations, where joint tasks can be performed.You can use it, when selecting Locations.

##### Library versions

You can choose which UJTL or DISR library version to use for your project. By default, libraries will be loaded, but the versions will not be set (except for the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool, where the latest library versions will be set).

There are the following ways of working with library versions:

- start a new project and work with the existing version of the libraries.
- start a new project with Cameo Enterprise Architecture and choose different version of the libraries (you can choose one of the existing or download using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD190'] ).
- open the existing project with libraries that are in the current modeling tool's version.

###### Choosing library version

When creating a new project, you can choose which library version you will be using.

- By default, the libraries version is not selected for NAF, NAF4, MODAF, UAF EA, and UAF projects.
- By default, the latest libraries version is selected for the DoDAF projects.

To choose which library version will be used

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a project' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
2. In the New Project dialog, General options, choose the needed version of the preferred library (see the figure below).
3. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing library version

You can change the library version at any time.

To change library version

1. In the Containment tree toolbar, click Options and choose Show Auxiliary Resources . [ATTACHMENT filename='containment_tree_toolbar.png']
2. In the Containment tree, right-click the library which version you want to change.
3. From the library shortcut menu, choose Change Library Version and then click the needed version. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_library_version.png']

If the selected library version does not contain elements of the previously used library version, such elements will be marked DELETED, but still remain in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

**Case Study**

Let us say, we need to add a standard or a policy for a system element using the StdV-1 Standards Profile table. We will select a standard from the DISR library.

To add a standard/policy from the DISR library

1. Create the StdV-1 diagram.
2. Create new or add an existing system element.
3. In the Standard / Policy cell of the created or added row, click the Edit button. The Select Standard dialog opens.
4. Click the DISR Library tab. The list of DISR standards is displayed.
5. Select the standard and do one of the following:
  - Double-click the selected standard to add it to the Selected elements list.
  - Click the + button to add the selected standard to the Selected elements list.
6. Click OK , after you finish selecting the standards.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1efa7c66-0a72-47fa-9bf3-79b1b37cd764"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715818604</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24cddb0a-7320-4e14-a02a-c97d0263fde0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715818606</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80e9bbef-7907-42e6-99db-469f683be2e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715818603</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="100c55c5-2e16-4c81-a155-4824f5b26970" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b211c3ff-32ab-4ff0-8422-de2aec19d0f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715818605</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>UAF plugin supports three element libraries:</p><ol><li>The Universal Joint Task List (UJTL).</li><li>DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry (DISR).</li><li>Joint Conditions.</li></ol><p>The <strong>Universal Joint Task List (UJTL)</strong>, when augmented with the Service task lists, is a comprehensive integrated menu of functional tasks, conditions, measures, and criteria. It supports all levels of the Department of Defense in executing the National Military Strategy. In UAF UJTL library consists of a list of Operational Activities representing universal joint tasks.</p><p>The <strong>DoD IT Standards Registry (DISR)</strong> is an online repository of IT standards formerly captured in the Joint Technical Architecture (JTA), Version 6.0. DISR replaces JTA. In UAF, DISR is the list of standard elements that any of UAF element may conform to. DISR Library usage is the same as UJTL library. Library item indexes are included when searching for elements within the element Selection dialog.</p><p>The <strong>Joint Conditions Library</strong> supports physical, military, and civil locations, where joint tasks can be performed.You can use it, when selecting Locations.</p><h3>Library versions</h3><p>You can choose which UJTL or DISR library version to use for your project. By default, libraries will be loaded, but the versions will not be set (except for the Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tool, where the latest library versions will be set).</p><p>There are the following ways of working with library versions:</p><ul><li>start a new project and work with the existing version of the libraries.</li><li>start a new project with Cameo Enterprise Architecture and choose different version of the libraries (you can choose one of the existing or download using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Resource Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource / Plugin Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</li><li>open the existing project with libraries that are in the current modeling tool's version.</li></ul><h4>Choosing library version</h4><p>When creating a new project, you can choose which library version you will be using.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81fe65a5-41e2-4aa8-b887-fd5bb2fac5fa"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="946dafee-e33f-4363-b8c2-2f5bed91821d">By default, the libraries version is not selected for </ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="946dafee-e33f-4363-b8c2-2f5bed91821d">NAF, NAF4, MODAF, UAF EA, and UAF projects.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="946dafee-e33f-4363-b8c2-2f5bed91821d">By default, the latest libraries version is selected for the DoDAF projects.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To choose which library version will be used</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog, <strong>General</strong> options, choose the needed version of the preferred library (see the figure below).</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_library_version.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using libraries" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Changing library version</h4><p>You can change the library version at any time.</p><p>To change library version</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree toolbar, click Options and choose <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment_tree_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using libraries" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the library which version you want to change.</li><li>From the library shortcut menu, choose <strong>Change Library Version</strong> and then click the needed version.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_library_version.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using libraries" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f97a5345-b0ad-4c0b-ad41-4f4ed65ecf78"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the selected library version does not contain elements of the previously used library version, such elements will be marked DELETED, but still remain in your model.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="marked_deleted.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using libraries" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="font-size: 16.0px;"><br /></strong></p><p><strong style="font-size: 16.0px;">Case Study</strong></p><p>Let us say, we need to add a standard or a policy for a system element using the StdV-1 Standards Profile table. We will select a standard from the DISR library.</p><p>To add a standard/policy from the DISR library</p><hr /><ol><li>Create the StdV-1 diagram.</li><li>Create new or add an existing system element.</li><li>In the Standard / Policy cell of the created or added row, click the <strong>Edit</strong> button. The<strong> Select Standard</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Click the <strong>DISR Library</strong> tab. The list of DISR standards is displayed.</li><li>Select the standard and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Double-click the selected standard to add it to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button to add the selected standard to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, after you finish selecting the standards.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting standards from DISR Library" ac:alt="Selecting standards from DISR Library"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_standard_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using libraries" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722900 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01135: Using SysML elements in UAF plugin

- page_id: `136722900`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722900/Using+SysML+elements+in+UAF+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:38.717+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['expand-sysml-toolbars', 'use-sysml-elements-in-updm-2-plugin']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML elements can be used to extend UAF provided set of concepts. SysML toolbars in UAF diagrams are collapsed by default.

For better understanding how to use SysML elements in UAF plugin models two case studies are described:

##### Case Study 1. The Requirement usage in OV-2 diagram****

We will use the Requirement element, which is a SysML element, in an OV-2 diagram.

To use the Requirement in an OV-2 diagram

1. Create an OV-2 diagram.
2. Create an Operational Performer in the diagram pane.
3. Expand the SysML Requirements Diagram toolbar and select Requirement.
4. Create it in the diagram pane.
5. Create the Satisfy relationship between the Operational Performer and the Requirement.

As you can see in the following figure, the architecture element satisfies the Requirement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Case Study 2. Describing a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model

The following example describes the actual situation, how you can use the SysML elements in the UAF model.

The case study uses the sample project Counting FTEs (requires CST).mdzip, which can be found in <MagicDraw installation folder>\samples\UAF.

Let us say, we need to describe a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model.

First, we need to create the typical organizational structure using OV-4 Organizational Relationship Chart diagram. The typical organizational structure defines types of departments and roles and how they are associated together in the modeled enterprise. The Department can contain any number of Employees and any number of sub Departments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Secondly, we generate an actual organizational chart based on the typical organizational chart (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='UAF12P2024x']). An actual organizational chart shows concrete departments and residing employees.

Each employee works a certain number of FTEs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Thirdly, we can use the SysML Parametric Diagram to describe the recursive FTE’s calculation for the selected department and each residing sub-department.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can execute the described calculations using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SysML elements can be used to extend UAF provided set of concepts. SysML toolbars in UAF diagrams are collapsed by default.</p><p>For better understanding how to use SysML elements in UAF plugin models two case studies are described:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42ca2c21-25d1-4a19-96ee-d57bf29dcfd5" /></p><h3>Case Study 1. The Requirement usage in OV-2 diagram<strong><br /></strong></h3><p>We will use the Requirement element, which is a SysML element, in an OV-2 diagram.</p><p>To use the Requirement in an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create an OV-2 diagram.</li><li>Create an Operational Performer in the diagram pane.</li><li>Expand the SysML Requirements Diagram toolbar and select Requirement.</li><li>Create it in the diagram pane.</li><li>Create the Satisfy relationship between the Operational Performer and the Requirement.</li></ol><p>As you can see in the following figure, the architecture element satisfies the Requirement.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="using_SysML.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Case Study 2. Describing a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model</h3><p>The following example describes the actual situation, how you can use the SysML elements in the UAF model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41d943c4-9265-4d20-af6c-51837017cc52"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The case study uses the sample project Counting FTEs (requires CST).mdzip, which can be found in &lt;MagicDraw installation folder&gt;\samples\UAF.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Let us say, we need to describe a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UAF model.</p><p>First, we need to create the typical organizational structure using OV-4 Organizational Relationship Chart diagram. The typical organizational structure defines types of departments and roles and how they are associated together in the modeled enterprise. The Department can contain any number of Employees and any number of sub Departments.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_typical_CST.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Secondly, we generate an actual organizational chart based on the typical organizational chart (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Instantiating structures" /></ac:link>). An actual organizational chart shows concrete departments and residing employees.</p><p>Each employee works a certain number of FTEs.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_actual_CST.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Thirdly, we can use the SysML Parametric Diagram to describe the recursive FTE’s calculation for the selected department and each residing sub-department.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parametric_diagram_in_UPDM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using SysML elements in UAF plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="581e272e-c0da-4542-a0ad-7f84f5214c6c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can execute the described calculations using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723533 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01137: Using To Do property

- page_id: `136723533`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723533/Using+To+Do+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:20.056+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked "added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.

To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property

1. From the main toolbar click Edit > Find TODO . All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the Search Results tab.

For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State *Searching (Aircraft)* before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability *Search* (source of the relationship).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked &quot;added to the To Do property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration is placed in the element's To Do property.</p><p>To find all elements which contain information in the To Do property</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main toolbar click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find TODO</strong>.<br />All elements which contain information in the To Do property are listed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4a2d810-8712-4983-8a2e-5d42a26d7ad6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you had Desired Effect relation connected to State <em>Searching (Aircraft)</em> before the migration, the information about it is stored in the To Do property of Capability <em>Search</em> (source of the relationship).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_of_capability_search.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Using To Do property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item.

- Benefit
- Cost
- KPI
- Loss
- Other
- Quality
- Revenue
- Time

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extensions**

Enumeration

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723412 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01141: Value Stream

- page_id: `136723412`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723412/Value+Stream
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:10.557+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extensions**

Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Instance Specification</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723414 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01142: Version Of Configuration

- page_id: `136723414`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723414/Version+Of+Configuration

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A property of a Whole Life Configuration, used in version control of a Versioned Element. It asserts that a Versioned Element is a version of a Whole Life Configuration.

**Architecture** **Framework**

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF, UAF

**Extension**

UML Property

It asserts that a Versioned Element is a version of a Whole Life Configuration.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0">MODAF, DoDAF, NAF, UAF</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Property </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723416 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01145: View

- page_id: `136723416`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An architecture view expresses the architecture of the system-of-interest in accordance with an architecture viewpoint (or simply, viewpoint).

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
