<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723503 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01146: View mapping

- page_id: `136723503`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723503/View+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:18.731+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and UAF 1.1.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.1 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Svcv-6 | SV-6 | The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 diagram, the SV-6 diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| Svcv-6 Role-based | SV-6 Role-based | The SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Role-based Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 Role-based diagram, the SV-6 Role-based diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| Service Channels Summary Table | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |
| Service Channels Summary Matrix | Deleted. | The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 2.1 and <span>UAF 1</span>.1.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 2.1</th><th><span>UAF 1</span>.1</th><th>Comments</th></tr><tr><td>Svcv-6</td><td>SV-6</td><td>The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 diagram, the SV-6 diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</td></tr><tr><td>Svcv-6 Role-based</td><td>SV-6 Role-based</td><td>The SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-6 Role-based Systems Resource Flow Matrix. Instead of SvcV-6 Role-based diagram, the SV-6 Role-based diagram is introduced and recommended to use.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Table</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Table is no longer used for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Channels Summary Matrix</td><td>Deleted.</td><td>The Service Channels Summary Matrix is no longer used for service channels analysis and creation. The Service Channel element is replaced to Resource Connector or Operational Connector.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723520 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01147: View Mappings

- page_id: `136723520`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723520/View+Mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:19.481+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes view mapping between UPDM 1.x and UPDM 2.0.

| UPDM 1.x | UPDM 2.0 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CV-3 | CV-2 | CV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the CV-3 Capability Phasing chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| CV-5 | CV-2 | CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the CV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The CV-5 tabular form of the representation is introduced and recommended to use. |
| StV-3 | StV-2 | StV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. Instead of the diagram, the StV-3 Capability Taxonomy chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| StV-5 | StV-2 | StV-5 Organization Deployment Mapping diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram. The StV-5 tabular form of representation is introduced and recommended to use. |
| PV-2 | PV-1 | PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram. Instead of the diagram, the PV-2 Project Timelines chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| AcV-2 | AcV-1 | AcV-2 Programme Timelines diagram is no longer used. An existing diagram will be loaded as the AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram. Instead of the diagram, the AcV-2 Acquisition Clusters chart is introduced and recommended to use. |
| SV-8 | SV-1 | SV-8 Capability Configuration Management diagram is refactored to meet UPDM 2.0 requirements. An existing diagram will be loaded as the SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification diagram. New form of the SV-8 diagram is introduced and recommended to use. |
| SvcV-1 | SOV-1 |  |
| SvcV-2 | SOV-2 |  |
| SvcV-3a | Service Channels Summary Matrix | Service Channels Summary Matrix is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis and creation. |
| SvcV-3b | SV-12 |  |
| SvcV-4a | SOV-5 structural |  |
| SvcV-4b | SOV-5 behavioral |  |
| SvcV-5 | Dependency Matrix |  |
| SvcV-6 | Service Channels Summary Table | Service Channels Summary Table is a new type of supportive tool, dedicated for service channels analysis, creation, and modification. |
| SvcV-7 | Generic Table |  |
| SvcV-10a | SOV-4a |  |
| SvcV-10b | SOV-4b |  |
| SvcV-10c | SOV-4c |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The CV-5 tabular form of the representation is introduced and recommended to use.</p></td></tr><tr><td>StV-3 </td><td>StV-2 </td><td><p>StV-3 Capability Phasing diagram is no longer used. ## PAGE 01148: Viewpoint

- page_id: `136723417`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723417/Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:10.793+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An architecture viewpoint frames (to formulate or construct in a particular style or language) one or more concerns. A concern can be framed by more than one viewpoint.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A concern can be framed by more than one viewpoint. </p><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Class</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

A type of comment that describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved.

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

**Extension**

UML Comment

## PAGE 01151: Whole Life Configuration Kind

- page_id: `136723420`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723420/Whole+Life+Configuration+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:10.957+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Whole Life Configuration. Its enumeration literals are:

- Service - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which Services are versioned.
- ResourcePerformer - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which ResourcePerformers are versioned.
- Organizational Resource - Indicates that the Whole Life Configuration associated with the Whole Life Configuration Kind is the master specification from which Organizational Resources are versioned.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Whole Life Configuration. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>Service - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which Services are versioned.</li><li>ResourcePerformer - Indicates that the WholeLifeConfiguration associated with the WholeLifeConfigurationKind is the master specification from which ResourcePerformers are versioned.</li><li>Organizational Resource - Indicates that the Whole Life Configuration associated with the Whole Life Configuration Kind is the master specification from which Organizational Resources are versioned.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 01152: Whole Life Enterprise

- page_id: `136723421`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723421/Whole+Life+Enterprise
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:11.009+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Whole Life Enterprise is a purposeful endeavor of any size involving people, organizations and supporting systems. It is made up of Temporal Parts and Structural Parts.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It is made up of Temporal Parts and Structural Parts. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721785 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01153: Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy

- page_id: `136721785`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721785/Working+with+A8+Standards+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:39.677+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Standards Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the A8 and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > A8 Standards Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for A8 Standards Taxonomy, and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Standard element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Standard (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_create_standard.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the A8 package, and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Standard (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Standard (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Standard (or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the diagram palette, choose Generalization.
3. Connect the Standard elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If needed, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Generalization.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Connect the Standard elements.</span></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="A8_standards_connect_generalization.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p>If needed, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.</p><p><br /></p><p>To make an element abstract</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Make sure that <strong>All</strong> is chosen for <strong>Properties</strong>.</li><li>Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_is_abstract.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To view the inherited properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="233"><ri:attachment ri:filename="inherited_measurements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>      <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721794 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01154: Working with A8 Standards Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `136721794`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721794/Working+with+A8+Standards+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:39.822+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Standards Taxonomy Table (A8)**provides all the available technical and non-technical standards, guidances, and policies.

To add a new Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard in the Standards Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.

To add an existing standard in the Standards Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Standard dialog, choose the desired Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> </span><strong>Standards Taxonomy Table (A8)</strong><span> </span>provides all the available technical and non-technical standards, guidances, and policies.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard in the Standards Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing standard in the Standards Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Standard </strong>dialog, choose the desired Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722397 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01155: Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table

- page_id: `136722397`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722397/Working+with+Actual+Project+Milestone+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:14.192+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The** **Actual** **Project** **Milestone Summary** **Table (Pj-Rm)** depicts all actual project milestones in the model.

Adding the actual project milestones into the table, you can see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table.

To create a table

1. In the Model Browser, select the owner for the new table.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - On the main toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Open the Content diagram and on its palette, click the Create Diagram button.
3. In the opened window, search for Actual Project Milestone Summary Table . An empty table is created.

When you have a table, you can start filling it out. Here you can add new or already existing in the model elements.

To add new Actual Project Milestone

1. In the table, click the Add New button. The new Actual Projet Milestone is created.
2. Fill out the needed columns.

To add existing model elements

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the table toolbar, click **Add Existing**. In the open **Select Actual Project Milestone**dialog, choose which elements you need to add to the table. If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.
  - In the Containment tree, select Actual Project Milestone and drag it to the table.
2. Click **OK** when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>The</strong> <strong>Actual</strong> <strong>Project</strong> <strong>Milestone Summary</strong> <strong>Table (Pj-Rm)</strong> depicts all actual project milestones in the model.</p><p>Adding the actual project milestones into the table, you can see their name, kind, start date, project, project status, version released, version withdrawn, and actual resource information. You will also be able to add or modify the actual project milestone information directly in this table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, select the owner for the new table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Press Ctrl+N.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Create Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Project Milestone Summary Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span> .</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Open the Content diagram and on its palette, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened window, search for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="297c4c37-d037-447b-813d-084eb89828b1"> Actual Project Milestone Summary Table</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br />An empty table is created.</li></ol><p>When you have a table, you can start filling it out. Here you can add new or already existing in the model elements.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3cdcbc4d-8f66-463e-b4d7-372b6d650fc7">To add new Actual Project Milestone</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new Actual Projet Milestone is created.</li><li>Fill out the needed columns.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add existing model elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. In the open <strong>Select Actual Project Milestone </strong>dialog, choose which elements you need to add to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af0cc9ad-aae0-4c41-9090-ab112a1b858b"><ac:rich-text-body>If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>In the Containment tree, select Actual Project Milestone and drag it to the table.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722432 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01156: Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `136722432`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722432/Working+with+Actual+Projects+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:15.315+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282638040

1282638053

1282638043

**Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1282638039

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Actual Projects to Capabilities Mapping Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4534a74b-7d1d-459d-8539-0857b4ec5dec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282638039</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01157: Working with Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix

- page_id: `136722487`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722487/Working+with+Actual+Resource+Taxonomy+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:17.909+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Actual Resources > Actual Resources Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1283014949

1283014961

1283014951

The **Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix** (Ar-Tx) describes the mapping between the actual responsible resources and the actual responsibilities.

The Rows of this matrix are Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organization, Actual Person, Actual Post) and the Columns are Actual Responsibility.

To create an Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (Actual Responsible Resources);
2. Specify the Columns Scope (Actual Responsibility);
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Responsible Resources map to Actual Responsibility through the Responsible For relationship.

To map the Actual Responsible Resource to Actual Responsibility

- Double-click the empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relationship

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1283014948

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722481 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01158: Working with Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `136722481`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722481/Working+with+Actual+Resources+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:17.426+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Actual Resources > Actual Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actual Resources Connectivity diagram is used to model typical and actual forms of the organizational relationships.

To create an typical Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, andPersons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an actual Actual Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship. You can display:the Competenceof the Organizational Resources.the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Actual Resources Connectivity diagram is used to model typical and actual forms of the organizational relationships.</p><p>To create an typical Actual Resources Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Organizations, </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an actual Actual Resources Connectivity diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Persons</span>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Relate Actual Organizational Resources with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Organization Relationship</span></span>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

To create an actual Actual Resources Structure diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722435 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01160: Working with Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `136722435`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722435/Working+with+Actual+Responsible+Resources+to+Actual+Projects+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:15.431+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282670460

1282670463

1282670462

The Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, andActual Posts) for Projects.

The rows in the Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix represent Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To create an Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Responsible Resources map to Actual Projects using the Responsible For relationship.

##### Mapping an Actual Responsible Resource to an Actual Project

To map an Actual Responsible Resource to an Actual Project

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282670459

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721857 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01161: Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart

- page_id: `136721857`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721857/Working+with+Actual+Strategic+Phases+Gantt+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:44.598+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1139450539

1139450543

1139450542

The Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart shows how Actual Strategic Phases change over time. The rows represent the Actual Strategic Phase (Enterprise Mission,Actual Enduring Task,Whole Life Enterprise,Actual Enterprise Phase,Value Stream) in the roadmap. The bars represent the related information of the Actual Strategic Phase.

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024x'] chapter.

##### Creating Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart

When you choose to create an Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']** opens.

To create an Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart from the wizard

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Strategic Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_actual_enterprise_phases_gantt_chart.png']
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart** check box.

To create an Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']** is disabled

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Strategic Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart .
2. Type the chart name. (Optional)
3. Click Finish .

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

##### Adding Actual Strategic Phase

To add an Actual Strategic Phase

1. On the Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New and choose one of the following:
  - Add New Enterprise Mission
  - Add New Actual Enduring Task
  - Add New Whole Life Enterprise
  - Add New Actual Enterprise Phase
  - **Add New Value Stream**
2. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**
3. Click Finish when you are done.

To add an existing Actual Strategic Phase

1. On the Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**
3. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Adding Sub Actual Strategic Phase

To add a new Sub Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Gantt chart tree, select the Actual Strategic Phase for which you want to create a Sub Actual Strategic Phase.
2. In the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Strategic Phase, click Add New Sub Actual Strategic Phase .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

To add an existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Gantt chart tree, select the Actual Strategic Phase for which you want to add an existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase.
2. In the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Strategic Phase, click Add Existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

When you choose to add new or existing Actual Strategic Phases, they will be listed for selection depending on the defined structure.

##### Displaying all suppressed or deleted from the chart

When an Actual Strategic Phase is displayed in *italic*, it means that some sub-elements were removed from the chart. You can restore them back with the click of one button.

To display all sub-elements removed from the chart

1. Select the Actual Strategic Phase which sub-elements you want to restore.
2. In the Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

The **Display All** button also expands the suppressed structure.

##### Deleting elements

To delete an element from the chart

1. Select an element.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete an element from the model

1. Select an element.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Deleting sub-elements

To delete sub-elements from the chart

1. Select the sub-element.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete sub-elements from the model

1. Select a sub-element.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

1139450538

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721895 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01162: Working with Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136721895`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721895/Working+with+Actual+Strategic+Phases+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:46.435+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1283102180

1283102192

1283102182

**The Actual Strategic Phases to** **Capabilities** **Mapping Matrix (St-Tr)** describes the mapping between the required capabilities and the actual strategic phases that those actual strategic phases support directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024x'].

The Rows of this matrix are Actual Strategic Phases and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create an Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Strategic Phases);
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Actual Strategic Phases map to Capabilities using Phases relationship.

To map an Actual Strategic Phase to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1283102179

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

Capabilities using Phases relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map an Actual Strategic Phase to a Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c47b4004-9d0b-4ff3-a0fc-0c337c5b46ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1283102179</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721192 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01163: Working with AcV-2 diagram

- page_id: `136721192`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721192/Working+with+AcV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:54.077+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint > MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413746516

413746518

413746514

**On this page**

3

413746517

##### Creating AcV-2 diagram

When you choose to create AcV-2 diagram, AcV-2 Creation Wizard opens by default.

To create an AcV-2 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click AcV-2 Project Timeliness .
3. In the AcV-2 Project Timeliness dialog, click Add . AcV-2 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner for the diagram. Click Next .
5. Add the project(s). Click Next .
6. Set the chart properties.
7. Click **Finish**. If you do not wish to use the AcV-2 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create AcV-2”.Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the **Environment Options** dialog go to **UAF** tab. In the **General** properties, set **Show PV-2 / AcV-2 / NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart** to False.

To create an AcV-2 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click AcV-2 Project Timeliness .
3. In the AcV-2 Project Timeliness dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.
5. Click OK . The blank AcV-2 diagram is created.

You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.

##### Adding actual projects to AcV-2 diagram

You can add a new or an existing actual project to the AcV-2 diagram.

To add a new actual project

1. On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.
2. Specify a time frame for the actual project.

To add a new actual sub project

1. Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.

To add an existing actual project

1. On the AcV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

##### Deleting actual projects from AcV-2 diagram

To delete an actual project from the chart

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete From Chart**. Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.

To delete an actual project from the model

1. Select an actual project. 
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete**.

##### Relating actual projects in AcV-2 diagram

You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.

You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate actual projects

1. Select two actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between actual projects

1. Select two related actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None.

To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Available validation suggestions

If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.

##### Adding Milestones in AcV-2 diagram

To add a milestone

1. Select an actual project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kind.
4. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens.
5. Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click Finish when you are done.

##### Deleting milestones in AcV-2 diagram

To delete a milestone

1. Select a milestone.
2. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Delete .

##### Applying actual project status in AcV-2 diagram

To apply the actual project status to a new milestone

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the actual project status by following steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

To edit the actual project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

Specify Project Status

##### Removing actual project status in AcV-2 diagram

To remove the project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. In the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] , click the Remove project status indicator mark button.
3. Click Finish when you are done.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721811 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01164: Working with Architecture Management diagram

- page_id: `136721811`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721811/Working+with+Architecture+Management+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:41.382+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Architecture Management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Architecture Management diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select any of the Architecture Management view specification packages (except Architecture Management Information) and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Architecture Management . [ATTACHMENT filename='architecture_diagram_new.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Architecture Management and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service Architecture element, but the same is valid for other available elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service Architecture (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_viewpoint_element.png']
2. Name the element.

Creating elements from the Containment tree differs from creating them in the diagram. The element availability depends on which Architecture Management view specification package you choose. In this example, the Service Architecture element creation from the Architecture Management Traceability package in the Containment tree is described, but any other element from a different package can be created in the same way.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Architecture Management Traceability package, and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Service Architecture (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_service_architecture.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability Configuration (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Service Architecture(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the Service Architecture will be connected to the Actual Enterprise Phase using the Implements relationship.

To connect the elements with the Implements relationship

1. Select an element from which the Implements will be drawn.
2. From the diagram palette, choose Implements.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create an Architecture Management diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select any of the Architecture Management view specification packages (except Architecture Management Information) and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Architecture Management</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="363" ac:width="322"><ri:attachment ri:filename="architecture_diagram_new.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Architecture Management diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Architecture Management</strong><strong> </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. An example is described using the Service Architecture element, but the same is valid for other available elements.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service Architecture </span>(or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="264" ac:width="618"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_viewpoint_element.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Architecture Management diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating elements from the Containment tree differs from creating them in the diagram. The element availability depends on which Architecture Management view specification package you choose. In this example, the Service Architecture element creation from the Architecture Management Traceability package in the Containment tree is described, but any other element from a different package can be created in the same way.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an element in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right-click the Architecture Management Traceability package, and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Search for the Service Architecture </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or another appropriate element)</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="361" ac:width="291"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_architecture.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Architecture Management diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="272" ac:width="1063"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_service_architecture.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Architecture Management diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Configuration</span> (or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service Architecture</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the Service Architecture will be connected to the Actual Enterprise Phase using the Implements relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Implements relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Implements will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the diagram palette, choose Implements.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="345" ac:width="778"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connect_elements_implements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Architecture Management diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721509 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01165: Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `136721509`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721509/Working+with+C1+Capability+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:18.098+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)** provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.

To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)</strong> provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720538 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01166: Working with CV-5 typical table

- page_id: `136720538`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720538/Working+with+CV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:36.960+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

627926007

627926020

627931615

**On this page**

627926010

##### Creating CV-5 typical table

The context of the CV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the CV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the CV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the CV-5 typical table table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].

###### emptyCreate a CV-5 typical table

To create an empty CV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more CV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a CV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a CV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627926006

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721820 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01167: Working with Date and Time Summary Table

- page_id: `136721820`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721820/Working+with+Date+and+Time+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:42.190+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Summary and Overview
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1726920497

1726920518

1726920508

The **Date and Time Summary Table (Sm-Ov)** depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.

Adding the date-time elements into the table, you can see their defined values, owners, and the list of the elements in which the date-time elements are used as tagged values.

To add existing model elements

1. In the table toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table. If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.
3. Click **OK** when you are done.

The selected date-time elements are added as rows.

1726920495

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2f84e27-9737-4e06-bc96-c0c095bfc824"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920497</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="734ce300-ae23-4361-82a6-4c5918666512"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920518</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8ea68af-f4e8-496f-ba0d-9b3d65d5df7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920508</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Date and Time Summary Table (Sm-Ov)</strong> depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.</p><p>Adding the date-time elements into the table, you can see their defined values, owners, and the list of the elements in which the date-time elements are used as tagged values.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add existing model elements</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select ISO8601 Date Time</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b7b60e6-1344-45fe-bb0b-299147c13860"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><p>The selected date-time elements are added as rows.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8290fc10-3a7c-4366-8b93-3f90c517d309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01168: Working with Dictionary diagram

- page_id: `136721802`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721802/Working+with+Dictionary+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:40.300+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Architecture Management > Architecture Management Information
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Dictionary (Am-If)** provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.

To create a new or add an existing term

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Dictionary (Am-If)</strong> provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new or add an existing term</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722490 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01169: Working with Environment diagram

- page_id: `136722490`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722490/Working+with+Environment+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:18.178+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Parameters Pm
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

380372338

380372340

1342655392

**On this page**

380372339

The **Environment** diagram shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.

The Environment diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)

##### Creating a diagram

An example is shown with the Operational Parameters, but the Environment diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.

To create an Environment diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Parameters and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Environment**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Environment**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Location element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Location in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Location and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Location in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Operational Structure (or Taxonomy, Connectivity, Constraints) package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Location and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_location.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connect the Location

When you have the Location created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

380372336

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Performable Under Condition' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Condition' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Environment' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722291 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01170: Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `136722291`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722291/Working+with+Functions+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:08.613+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282779718

1282779730

1282779720

The rows of the Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

##### Creating Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

To create a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

##### Mapping Function to Operational Activity in Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

1282779717

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722845 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01171: Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table

- page_id: `136722845`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722845/Working+with+hierarchical+Actual+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:36.392+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: TIP

741994468

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: TIP

741994480

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: TIP

741994470

The hierarchical table for actual measurements allows working with the actual measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing Actual Property Sets and define values. When you add a Property Set to the table, all the existing Actual Property Sets will be collected recursively according to the defined scope.

For your convenience, it is advised to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x'] first.

To create <domain name> Actual Measurements Table

- Do one of the following:
  - Right-click the <domain name> Parameters package and choose Create Diagram > <domain name> Actual Measurements Table .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select **<domain name> Actual Measurements Table**.
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']Typical Measurements Table's]]> toolbar, click the **Evaluate Measurements** button.

To work with <domain name> Actual Measurements Table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Add New** button and from the **Select Element** dialog, choose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024x']. The appropriate Property Sets are collected to the Select Element dialog based on the defined scope and whether they have measurements. The new Actual Property Sets with their corresponding structure recursively are created.
  - Click the **Add Existing**button and from the **Select Element** dialog, choose existing Property Sets. In the **Select Element** dialog, elements are offered according to the selected perspective - Type, Instance, or Any element.If the Type radio button is selected, all the appropriate Property Sets with instances and are not added in the table yet.If the Instance radio button is selected, all the appropriate instances according to the Property Set scope and not added to the table yet. The existing Actual Property Sets are added to the table with their corresponding structure recursively.
2. Double-click the Measure, Start Date, or End Date cell to add desired values. You can also choose to add more columns to the table.

#### TIP: TIP

TIP

You can also drag and drop the Property Sets from the appropriate domain in the Containment tree. The Actual Property Sets will be collected to the table recursively.

To add missing measurements

- Do one of the following:
  - In the table, choose an element for which you want to add missing measurements and in the table toolbar, click Add Missing Measurements > Update Selected Actual Property Set . The existing measurements are added for the selected Actual Property Set if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for the selected Actual Property Set.
  - In the table toolbar, click Add Missing Measurements > Update All Actual Property Sets . The existing measurements are added for all the Actual Property Sets if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for all the Actual Property Sets.

741994466

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']
