Actual Measurements Table</strong>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the main toolbar, click </span><strong>Create Diagram</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select <strong>&lt;domain name&gt; Actual Measurements Table</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[<domain name> Typical Measurements Table's]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> toolbar, click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To work with &lt;domain name&gt; Actual Measurements Table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and from the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, choose <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Property Set elements for measurement tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Property Sets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8853d02b-70cb-4427-a63d-c0900fbfbda7"><ac:rich-text-body>The appropriate Property Sets are collected to the Select Element dialog based on the defined scope and whether they have measurements.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The new Actual Property Sets with their corresponding structure recursively are created.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and from the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, choose existing Property Sets.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7be02df9-e9fd-46f7-9fc7-5171e1f774a1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, elements are offered according to the selected perspective - Type, Instance, or Any element.</p><ul><li>If the Type radio button is selected, all the appropriate Property Sets with instances and are not added in the table yet.</li><li>If the Instance radio button is selected, all the appropriate instances according to the Property Set scope and not added to the table yet.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The existing Actual Property Sets are added to the table with their corresponding structure recursively.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>Double-click the Measure, Start Date, or End Date cell to add desired values. You can also choose to add more columns to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fcc78b1-4222-4d8c-92fd-54f2c1c76fb5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">TIP</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0b69bf61-75e6-49c8-b201-3d7fc10c7773">You can also drag and drop the Property Sets from the appropriate domain in the Containment tree. The Actual Property Sets will be collected to the table recursively.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To add missing measurements</p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the table, choose an element for which you want to add missing measurements and in the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Missing Measurements</strong> &gt; <strong>Update Selected Actual Property Set</strong>.<br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1b72efe6-00e0-4256-9239-8e8d9e01c12a">The existing measurements are added for the selected Actual Property Set if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for the selected Actual Property Set.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Missing Measurements</strong> &gt; <strong>Update All Actual Property Sets</strong>.<br />The existing measurements are added for all the Actual Property Sets if the measurements already exist in the model but are not displayed in the table. If there are no measurements, they are created for all the Actual Property Sets.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="291d06aa-7aef-4930-a740-3cc00ba635e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">741994466</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Property Set elements for measurement tables" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related domains</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Personnel Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Projects Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resources Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Security Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Services Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Standards Parameters" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Summary and Overview" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="NSV-7 System Typical Quality Requirements Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="C7 Performance Parameters" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722839 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01172: Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `136722839`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722839/Working+with+hierarchical+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:36.110+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: TIP

742058609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: TIP

742058622

939865744

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: TIP

742058611

The hierarchical table for typical measurements allows working with the measurements directly in the table. You can add new or existing property sets and create measurements for them. Moreover, define measurement types, default values, and documentation directly in the table.

##### Working with Typical Measurements Table

To work with <domain name> Typical Measurements Table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Add New** button and from the drop-down list select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024x']. The appropriate Property Sets are collected to the drop-down list according to the domain in which the table is created.
  - Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Property Set. The new row in the table is created.
2. Select Property Set and in the table toolbar, click the Add New Measurement button.
3. Double-click the Type, Default Value, or Documentation cell add the desired measurement to specify it. You can also choose to add more columns to the table.

#### INFO: TIP

TIP

You can also drag and drop the Property Sets from the appropriate domain from the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An earlier version of the table for Typical Measurements vs. new hierarchical table for Typical Measurements

##### Display modes

There are three display modes available for the hierarchical Typical Measurements Table:

- Display Structure. Choose this mode to show all owned measurements in a structure view. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_structure.png']
- Display Structure with owned Measurements Recursively . Choose this mode to show all owned measurements recursively in a structure view. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_structure_owned_recursively.png']
- Display List of Measurements Recursively. Choose to show elements with all their measurements in a hierarchical order. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_mode_list_recursively.png']

To choose a display mode

1. In the hierarchical Typical Measurements Table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options.png'] .
2. In the shortcut menu, click Display Mode and then choose one of the available modes.

##### Evaluating measurements

After the Typical Measurements Table is created and filled out, you can create an Actual Measurements Table.

To create an Actual Measurements Table from the Typical Measurements Table

- In the filled-out Typical Measurements Table's toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. The hierarchical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x'] is created.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Typical Measurements Table.

To generate the report from the table toolbar

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

The report is generated with all the data displayed in the table.

To generate the report from the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the desired package.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Generate Report > UAF > <more> > Typical Measurements Excel Spreadsheet .
3. Define the name and select a location for your report.
4. Click Save .
5. In the open question dialog, choose whether you want to view the report after it is generated. If you choose to view it, the report is opened afterward.

In the report, the Property Sets with at least one measurement are collected from the chosen package.

If you need to include more packages as the report scope, then use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024x']**.

To generate the report from the Report Wizard

1. In the main menu, click Tools and then chose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024x']**Report Wizard**.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024x'] dialog, expand the UAF package and choose the Typical Measurements Table template.
3. Click Next .
4. Select the report data and click Next .
5. Select the desired scope (particular package or entire model).
6. Click Next .
7. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
8. Click Generate .

- If the chosen scope contains a single open hierarchical Typical Measurements table, the report is generated from the data of that table.
- If the chosen scope contains more than one open table, the generated report contains the data from all scope.

742058607

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721549 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01173: Working with L1 Node Types Table

- page_id: `136721549`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721549/Working+with+L1+Node+Types+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:22.758+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Node Types****Table (L1)**provides the definitions for all the operational concepts.

To add a new element in the Node Types table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose from the following:
  - Known Resource
  - Operational Architecture
  - Operational Mitigation
  - Operational Performer A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.

To add an existing operational concept in the Node Types table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Element dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer).
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> <strong>Node Types</strong></span><strong> Table (L1)</strong><span> </span>provides the definitions for all the operational concepts.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new element in the Node Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose from the following:<ul><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Architecture</li><li>Operational Mitigation</li><li>Operational Performer<br />A new row is added.</li></ul></li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing operational concept in the Node Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Select Element </strong>dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer).</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721614 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01174: Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `136721614`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721614/Working+with+L2-L3+Logical+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:26.426+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911370602

911370604

INLINE

478104274

INLINE

**On this page**

911370603

INLINE

##### Creating a diagram

To create an L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the L2-L3 package. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**L2-L3 LogicalTaxonomy**.
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **L2-L3 LogicalTaxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the L2-L3 package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connect the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

- A L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.
- The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

## PAGE 01175: Working with matrices

- page_id: `136722905`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722905/Working+with+matrices
- version_number: 1

- labels: ['reset', 'predefined', 'matrix', 'matrices']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282261837

1282261849

1653000796

**On this page**

1282261839

For more generic procedures on working with matrices, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024x'] chapter.

##### Building matrices

To build a matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope .
2. Specify the Column Scope .
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

##### Mapping elements in matrices

To map elements

- Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

##### Resetting to default

When working with the predefined matrices, you can make some changes to the default settings, e.g. change the row or columns element type, dependency criteria, directions. You can restore the following settings to default at any time:

- Description
- Direction
- Dependency Criteria
- Show Elements
- Column Element Type
- Row Element Type

To restore the default predefined matrix settings

- In the matrix toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options.png'] and choose the Reset to Default command.

1282261836
